evidence_tier_definitions.peer_reviewed: Published in a peer-reviewed journal, verified PMID/DOI.
evidence_tier_definitions.clinic_presentation: Clinic document, slide deck, or non-peer-reviewed manuscript. Usable with caveat.
evidence_tier_definitions.clinical_experience: Clinician-attributed statement without a traceable document. Cite with explicit framing.
evidence_tier_definitions.organizational_primary: FEMM/RHRI, Saint Paul VI, clinic fact sheet, or NeoFertility institutional document (not peer-reviewed article, not clinician-attributed). Cite with document name and date.

facts.claim: Infertility tests that should not be routinely ordered include laparoscopy for unexplained infertility (ACOG/ASRM)
facts.body: ACOG Committee Opinion (co-authored with ASRM) explicitly states that 'infertility tests that should not be routinely ordered include laparoscopy for unexplained infertility.' This is the formal recommendation that guides REI practice. Standard REI workup: history/physical, AMH, transvaginal ultrasound, serum progesterone, HSG or hysterosonography, hysteroscopy, semen analysis. No laparoscopy, no selective HSG, no cervical mucus evaluation. The Spirov REI textbook states cervical mucus 'rarely, if ever, is the sole or principal cause of infertility' and does not recommend post-coital testing. This recommendation directly contributes to the high rate of 'unexplained' infertility diagnoses that RRM resolves through more thorough evaluation (24% to 1% in Boyle's 187-couple cohort).

source.raw_source_id: ACOG Committee Opinion; ASRM; Spirov REI textbook; Whittaker NM, RRM vs ART lecture

facts.verification_notes: Promoted from local rrm-cli facts.json (170 curated facts)

facts.claim: IUI since 1700s; Clomid FDA 1967; REI fellowship 1972; IVF 1978; ICSI 1990s
facts.body: Key milestones in assisted reproductive technology: First child from artificial insemination born in the 1700s. IUI has been performed since the 1700s. Clomiphene citrate (Clomid) received FDA approval in 1967. Diagnostic laparoscopy gained popularity in the 1970s. REI recognized as an ABOG fellowship subspecialty in 1972. First IVF birth (Louise Brown) in 1978. ICSI (Intracytoplasmic Sperm Injection) developed in the 1990s. This timeline provides context for understanding that IVF is relatively recent (less than 50 years), while fertility-focused surgery and hormonal treatments have much longer histories.

source.raw_source_id: Dr. Naomi Whittaker IIRRM lecture (RRM vs ART); standard medical history references

facts.verification_notes: Promoted from local rrm-cli facts.json (170 curated facts)

facts.claim: ART 28.4% prematurity vs 11.2% natural conception; 24.7% direct effect independent of multiples
facts.body: Large-scale analysis shows ART newborns have a 28.4% prematurity rate vs. 11.2% for natural conception. Formal mediation analysis demonstrates that multiple gestations mediate 71.2% of the ART-prematurity effect, but a 24.7% 'direct effect' of ART on prematurity remains independent of multiple gestations. This means even singleton ART pregnancies carry elevated prematurity risk compared to natural conception. RRM singleton prematurity rates are 4.0-4.2% (NeoFertility cohorts), comparable to or better than natural conception rates.

source.raw_source_id: Mediation analysis studies; RRM comparison: Boyle et al., JRRM 2025

facts.verification_notes: Promoted from local rrm-cli facts.json (170 curated facts)

facts.claim: 58 live births for 162 couples, crude LBR 35.8% (abstract rounding states 35.4%); adjusted CLBR 57.4% at 24 months by Kaplan-Meier
facts.body: Retrospective case series of 162 couples at a hospital-based RRM clinic in Brisbane, Australia (2005-2010). Crude live birth rate 35.8% (58/162), Kaplan-Meier adjusted CLBR 57.4% at 24 months. Median time to conception 5.0 months. Median female age 33.7, median infertility duration 33.9 months. Zero twin deliveries, 5.2% preterm, 12.5% miscarriage.

source.raw_source_id: James G, McLindon LA, Hatch J, Mol BW, Turner JV. Pregnancy outcomes from a restorative infertility treatment model: a single centre case series. medRxiv. 2021. doi:10.1101/2021.04.14.21251044

facts.body: Among 74 couples with unexplained infertility (45.7% of cohort), 32 achieved live birth (crude 43.2%). Kaplan-Meier adjusted CLBR was 58.7% at 12 months and 71.2% at 24 months. facts.claim: Pregnancy was achieved by 240 couples (62.5%)
facts.body: Study of 384 women at 17 Australian Billings Ovulation Method clinics (1999-2003). 240 couples (62.5%) achieved pregnancy within 2 years. Mucus symptom observation stratified 'high pregnancy-potential' (72.3% conceived) from 'low pregnancy-potential' (44.4% conceived) groups, with a 30-percentage-point difference. Time to conception was 4.2 months (high) vs 6.4 months (low).

facts.body: IVF live birth rates vary significantly by underlying diagnosis (SART 2019 data): male factor has the highest IVF success rates, diminished ovarian reserve has the lowest. This underscores the value of accurate and detailed diagnosis, which RRM provides. Without thorough diagnosis, patients with treatable conditions may undergo IVF unnecessarily.

facts.claim: 6 diagnoses found after 16 years infertility and 8 failed IVF/ICSI transfers; live birth at 39 weeks
facts.body: A 35-year-old Croatian woman with 16 years of infertility, 3 recurrent miscarriages, and 8 unsuccessful IVF/ICSI embryo transfers (5 stimulated cycles, 5 fresh + 3 frozen transfers, 2008-2012) had 6 previously unidentified diagnoses found through RRM evaluation: (1) PCOS, (2) poor follicle function, (3) low endorphins, (4) hypothyroidism, (5) uterine septum (missed on IVF ultrasounds), (6) balanced Robertsonian translocation Ch 13-14. After surgical correction of the septum and 3 medicated RRM cycles, she had a full-term live birth at 39 weeks (3,670g male infant). Proposes up to 12 cycles of cycle optimization before IVF.

source.raw_source_id: Boyle PC, Stanford JB, Zecevic I. J Med Case Rep. 2022;16:246. doi:10.1186/s13256-022-03465-w

facts.claim: Hypoandrogenemia (DHEA-S 1.8, normal 2.7-9.2) identified as contributory factor for recurrent miscarriage; DHEA treatment led to live birth
facts.body: A 30-year-old Romanian woman with 5 recurrent miscarriages (including fetal demise at 24 weeks) and 1 prior live birth had hypoandrogenemia identified through RRM evaluation: testosterone <0.4 nmol/L (normal 0.3-1.7), free testosterone <0.5 (normal 0.3-5.6), DHEA-S 1.8 umol/L (normal 2.7-9.2). Standard recurrent miscarriage workup at a Dublin hospital found no cause. Treatment: DHEA 25mg twice daily pre-conception (continued 20mg during pregnancy), clomiphene, HCG, naltrexone. Conceived on 2nd cycle of targeted intercourse. Live birth at 36 weeks by C-section, 3,080g healthy boy. DHEA is a naturally occurring androgen precursor, not a synthetic hormone.

source.raw_source_id: Boyle PC, Pandalache C, Turczynski C. Front Med. 2024;11:1358563. doi:10.3389/fmed.2024.1358563

facts.verification_notes: Promoted from local rrm-cli facts.json (170 curated facts)

facts.claim: Only 22% normal mucus scores; 80% severe mucus issue in endo patients
facts.body: Pope Paul VI Institute data shows only 22% of endometriosis patients had normal mucus cycle scores. Approximately 80% had severe mucus issues (dry cycles or very low mucus). The cervical crypts are sensitive to inflammation from endometriosis and decrease secretion. Tail-end brown bleeding of 2-3+ days at the end of the period strongly suggests endometriosis or uterine inflammation (endometritis) -- brown bleeding indicates dead necrotic tissue from an unhealthy uterine lining caused by severe inflammation. One day of tail-end brown bleeding can be relatively normal; two or more days is considered abnormal. Conventional REI dismisses cervical mucus as 'rarely, if ever, the sole or principal cause of infertility' (Spirov textbook) and does not recommend post-coital testing, reasoning that IUI/IVF bypass the cervical factor anyway.

source.raw_source_id: Whittaker NM, Endometriosis Masterclass; Pope Paul VI Institute data; Spirov REI textbook

facts.claim: Approximately 30,000 new ovarian cancer cases are diagnosed and 15,000 women die annually in the US; 2% of US women are at lifetime risk; ovarian cancer accounts for 5% of female cancer deaths.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Approximately 30,000 new cases of ovarian cancer are diagnosed and 15,000 women die from this disease in a given year." Section: Ovarian Cancer.

facts.claim: The five-year survival rate for advanced-stage ovarian cancer is 25-30%, and 70-75% of patients present at this advanced stage.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The five-year survival rate for women with advanced stage disease is only 25 to 30 percent and 70 to 75 percent will present in this fashion." Section: Ovarian Cancer.

facts.claim: Babies born to women with breast cancer may have significantly lower birthweight and higher spontaneous abortion rates, but no increased risk of congenital anomalies; maternal survival appears unchanged.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "the birthweight of babies born to women with breast cancer may be significantly lower" and "the spontaneous abortion rate appears to be higher." Section: Final Note.

facts.claim: One in eight women in the United States will develop breast cancer during her lifetime; in 2002 there were 205,000 new cases and 40,000 deaths.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "One in eight women in the United States will develop breast cancer during her lifetime." and "new cases in 2002 was 205,000" Section: Breast Cancer.

facts.claim: The diagnosis of Stage I breast cancer increased 113 percent between 1983 and 1997 according to SEER data.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The diagnosis of Stage I breast cancer increased 113 percent between 1983 and 1997" Section: Breast Cancer.

facts.claim: Invasive lobular carcinoma accounts for 10 to 15 percent of invasive breast cancers and originates in the glandular tissue of the breast.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "invasive lobular carcinoma. This accounts for 10 to 15 percent of invasive breast cancers." Section: Breast Cancer.

facts.claim: Breast cancer is 100 times more common in women than in men; women with personal history of breast cancer are 3-4 times more likely to have a recurrence.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "It is 100 times more common in women." and "women with a history of breast cancer are three to four times more likely to have a recurrence" Section: Breast Cancer.

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The most common location for small bowel endometriosis is the terminal ileum." Section: Small Bowel (Terminal Ileum).

facts.claim: If bowel lumen is entered during excision of terminal ileum endometriosis, superficial serosal closure is inadequate and a general surgeon must be consulted.

source.title: Chapter 71: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the Gynecologist
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "If excision does not enter the bowel lumen, this closure is adequate. If lumen is entered, consult a general surgeon" Section: Technique for Superficial Lesions.

facts.claim: When removing and imbricating epiploic fat tags containing endometriosis on the rectosigmoid colon, the arteriole feeding the fat tag must be securely ligated.

source.title: Chapter 71: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the Gynecologist
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Remember there is an arteriole feeding the fat tag. When removing and imbricating, the vessel must be securely ligated." Section: Closure.

facts.claim: Approximately 20 percent of appendiceal endometriosis lesions are detected only by palpation and may be missed by visual inspection alone, which is the rationale some cite for incidental appendectomy in patients with severe endometriosis.

source.title: Chapter 71: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the Gynecologist
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "approximately 20 percent of appendiceal lesions are detected only by palpation and may be missed by visual inspection alone." Section: Appendectomy.

facts.claim: Bowel endometriosis lesions covered in PEARS techniques are generally considered mild, but may cause considerable pain and may contribute to infertility or miscarriages.

source.title: Chapter 71: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the Gynecologist
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Each lesion presented would generally be considered mild, but may cause considerable pain and may contribute to infertility or miscarriages." Section: Procedures Covered.

facts.claim: Appendiceal blood supply comes from terminal branches of the ileocolic artery or branches from the anastomotic arcades, and there may be multiple appendiceal arteries within the mesoappendix that must be secured during appendectomy.

source.title: Chapter 71: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the Gynecologist
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Comes from terminal branches of the ileocolic artery or branches from the anastomotic arcades" Section: Appendiceal Blood Supply.

facts.claim: Bowel endometriosis surgery requires a team approach including a general or colorectal surgeon; the gynecologic surgeon should not attempt these procedures alone.

source.title: Chapter 72: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the General Surgeon
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "These complex surgical procedures require involvement of a general or colorectal surgeon as part of the team treating patients with endometriosis." Section: Team Approach Required.

facts.claim: After stapled small bowel anastomosis, the staple line is oversewn with 3-0 Vicryl inverting Lembert sutures and the mesenteric defect closed with two interrupted 3-0 Vicryl sutures.

source.title: Chapter 72: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the General Surgeon
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Oversew staple line with 3-0 Vicryl inverting Lembert sutures" Section: Case Example.

facts.claim: The sigmoid colon terminates at the rectosigmoid junction at the S-3 vertebral level, and its mesentery originates where the ureter and ovarian vessels cross the pelvic brim.

source.title: Chapter 72: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the General Surgeon
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Terminates at rectosigmoid junction at S-3" Section: Sigmoid Colon.

facts.claim: The rectum is generally 10-15 cm long and contains internal rectal valves (Houston) that segregate it into 3-cm sections.

source.title: Chapter 72: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the General Surgeon
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Generally 10-15 cm long, terminates at anus" and "Internal rectal valves (Houston) segregate into 3-cm sections" Section: Rectum.

facts.claim: Penetrating anterior rectal endometriosis nodules can often be enucleated because only a small percentage penetrate through the mucosa; staying submucosal preserves bowel lumen integrity.

source.title: Chapter 72: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the General Surgeon
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.claim: EEA stapled rectosigmoid anastomosis typically uses a 28- or 31-mm EEA stapler, with a 2-0 Prolene purse-string placed 2-3 mm from the bowel edge.

source.title: Chapter 72: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the General Surgeon
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Insert sizing instruments; determine largest appropriate size (usually 28- or 31-mm EEA)" and "Place 2-0 Prolene purse-string suture 2-3 mm from bowel edge" Section: EEA Stapled Anastomosis.

facts.claim: After EEA firing, the two circular tissue doughnuts must be inspected for circumferential integrity and sent to pathology to confirm complete anastomosis.

source.title: Chapter 72: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the General Surgeon
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Evaluate two circular doughnuts for integrity (send to pathology)" Section: EEA Stapled Anastomosis.

facts.claim: Rectosigmoid anastomoses are leak-tested by filling the pelvis with warm Ringer's lactate and distending the anastomosis with air via a proctoscope or Foley catheter; air bubbles indicate a leak.

source.title: Chapter 72: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the General Surgeon
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Fill pelvis with warm Ringer's lactate" and "Air bubbles = leak present" Section: Leak Testing.

facts.claim: If a leak is detected at the rectosigmoid anastomosis and is inaccessible for reinforcement, the anastomosis must be redone or a proximal diverting colostomy performed.

source.title: Chapter 72: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the General Surgeon
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "If inaccessible, anastomosis must be redone or proximal diverting colostomy performed." Section: Leak Testing.

facts.claim: Rectal mucosa that appears dark or blackened at surgery is typically melanosis coli from chronic bowel stimulant use, not endometriosis involving mucosa.

source.title: Chapter 72: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the General Surgeon
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "This is NOT endometriosis involving mucosa but rather melanosis coli from chronic use of bowel stimulants." Section: Melanosis Coli.

facts.claim: Coronado et al. reported satisfactory pain relief and improved fertility rates following anterior wall resection for deeply infiltrating colorectal endometriosis.

source.title: Chapter 72: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the General Surgeon
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Coronado et al reported satisfactory pain relief following anterior wall resection for deeply infiltrating colorectal endometriosis." Section: Clinical Presentations.

facts.claim: After bowel resection for endometriosis, bowel recuperation takes approximately 4-5 days; patients advance from clear liquids to full diet once flatus returns.

source.title: Chapter 72: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the General Surgeon
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "4-5 days for bowel recuperation" and "Once flatus returns → clear liquids → advance to full general diet" Section: Recovery Timeline.

facts.claim: Patients are discharged after bowel endometriosis surgery once they tolerate at least 2 general diet meals with continued flatus passage.

source.title: Chapter 72: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the General Surgeon
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Discharge after tolerating ≥2 general diet meals with continued flatus passage" Section: Recovery Timeline.

facts.claim: During sigmoid colon mobilization, care must be taken not to injure the ureter and ovarian vessels, which cross the left common iliac vessels near where sigmoid vessels enter the mesentery.

source.title: Chapter 72: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the General Surgeon
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.claim: Resection and reanastomosis of the rectosigmoid colon is indicated when endometriosis lesions are so extensive they cause bowel lumen stricture.

source.title: Chapter 72: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the General Surgeon
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Indicated when lesions are so extensive they cause bowel lumen stricture." Section: Resection and Reanastomosis of the Rectosigmoid Colon.

facts.claim: During rectosigmoid mobilization, middle hemorrhoidal vessels are identified, clamped, divided, and ligated with 0 Vicryl ties before distal rectal transection with a 55-mm TA reticulating device.

source.title: Chapter 72: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the General Surgeon
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Identify, clamp, divide, ligate middle hemorrhoidal vessels with 0 Vicryl" Section: Case Example.

facts.claim: The small bowel ileum is characterized as narrower with thinner walls than the jejunum and is generally retroperitoneal with anterior peritoneal attachment to the posterior abdominal wall.

source.title: Chapter 72: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the General Surgeon
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.claim: Hand-sewn two-layer small bowel anastomosis produces a solid watertight closure but requires extensive tissue handling, and reactive sutures may narrow the bowel lumen through imbrication and scarring.

source.title: Chapter 72: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the General Surgeon
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Reactive sutures may narrow bowel lumen through imbrication and scarring." Section: Hand-Sewn Small Bowel Anastomosis.

facts.claim: Endometriosis of the anterior rectum can cause complete obliteration of the cul-de-sac, leading many gynecologists to consider these cases non-surgical.

source.title: Chapter 72: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the General Surgeon
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

source.title: Chapter 72: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the General Surgeon
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.claim: Stapled small bowel anastomosis is the technique of choice because it preserves small vessel patency and produces exceptional bowel lumen compared to hand-sewn methods.

source.title: Chapter 72: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the General Surgeon
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

source.title: Chapter 72: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the General Surgeon

source.authors: Hilgers TW

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.claim: Only 2 RCTs (86 women) for IVF vs expectant management; zero IVF RCTs for PCOS or endometriosis
facts.body: Cochrane 2023 review found only 2 RCTs (86 women total) comparing IVF to expectant management for unexplained infertility. Zero IVF RCTs exist for PCOS (2023 PCOS International Guideline) or endometriosis (ESHRE 2022). This is the factual basis for the 'RCT double standard' guardrail: IVF itself lacks the RCT evidence it demands from RRM.

facts.claim: True non-obstructive (secretive) azoospermia prevalence varies by denominator: (1) among men in infertility workup (equivalently, among all couples presenting with infertility), pooled prevalence is ~1.2% [95% CI 0.7-1.8%]; (2) among men with any diagnosed male factor infertility, ~1.9% [95% CI 1.2-2.9%]; (3) among men with isolated male factor (sole identified cause), ~6.0% [95% CI 3.6-9.8%]. Sources: Grande 2025 (n=1,014 couples, 14 NOA) and Hilgers 1997 (n=538 infertility men, 6 azoospermic with literature 65% NOA adjustment).

source.title: Comprehensive diagnostic and therapeutic approach to male factor infertility aimed at natural fertility: A multicentric retrospective cohort study.
source.authors: Grande G, Garolla A, Graziani A, Astorri AL, Cammarota MV, Merola A, Polidori MP, Lulli E, Busato E, Pesce F, Pompa G, Pontecorvi A, Milardi D, Ferlin A

facts.verification_notes: Derived meta-analytic estimates calculated 2026-04-19. Denominators: (A) all infertile couples / all men in workup: Grande 14/1014 + Hilgers 4/538 NOA-adjusted = 18/1552 Wilson 95% CI 0.73-1.83%. (B) men with any male factor: Grande 14/689 (233 isolated + 456 combined) + Hilgers 4/277 abnormal semen = 18/966 Wilson 95% CI 1.18-2.93%. (C) men with isolated male factor: Grande 14/233 Wilson 95% CI 3.61-9.83% (Hilgers lacks couple-level stratification). Hilgers NOA fraction from AUA Best Practice Statement midpoint 65% of total azoospermia. Choice of denominator must match the clinical question: patient-facing 'how likely is azoospermia the cause' uses (A); 'given male factor how often is it NOA' uses (B); 'given male factor is sole cause how often NOA' uses (C).

facts.claim: Endometriosis diagnostic delay: commonly up to 10 years from symptom onset to diagnosis; systematic reviews report 7-10 years across healthcare systems (De Corte 2024); Pugsley 2007 reported a 9-year median in UK general practice.
facts.body: Time from symptom onset to endometriosis diagnosis commonly stretches to ten years. De Corte et al. (BJOG 2024, PMID 39373298) systematically reviewed 17 studies and documented delays of 7-10 years across healthcare systems, with diagnosis times ranging from 5 to 12 years overall. Pugsley & Ballard (BJGP 2007, PMID 17550672) reported a median of 9 years from first symptomatic presentation to diagnosis in UK general practice. The delay has not uniformly improved across the past two decades.

source.title: Management of endometriosis in general practice: the pathway to diagnosis.
source.journal: The British journal of general practice : the journal of the Royal College of General Practitioners
source.authors: Pugsley Z, Ballard K

facts.verification_notes: Linked via WS1 citation resolution (2026-04-06)

facts.claim: 40-60% recurrence for ablation within 1-2 years; 75-85% long-term relief with excision
facts.body: Endometriosis surgery outcomes differ dramatically by technique. Ablation/non-excisional surgery has a 40-60% recurrence risk within 1-2 years, even with concurrent birth control or Lupron suppression. Excision surgery achieves approximately 75-85% long-term relief. Most 'recurrence' after surgery is actually persistence of disease from incomplete surgery (per Jeff Arrington, Endometriosis Center). Women with endometriomas are most likely to have true recurrence, likely due to hidden microscopic disease in the ovary. Suppressive medications (Lupron, birth control) given 3+ months before surgery can change the appearance of endo implants, making them harder to visualize and excise completely.

source.raw_source_id: Whittaker NM, Endometriosis Masterclass; Arrington J, Endometriosis Center

facts.verification_notes: Promoted from local rrm-cli facts.json (170 curated facts)

facts.claim: All aspects of conception are potentially affected: egg, sperm, cervical mucus, endometrium
facts.body: Endometriosis affects every stage of conception: (1) Egg quality -- ovaries sitting in inflammatory fluid impair egg development. (2) Sperm -- damaged by swimming through inflamed uterus. (3) Cervical mucus -- decreased quality/quantity (80% of endo patients). (4) Implantation -- inflammatory cytokines impair implantation; endometriosis creates decoy progesterone receptors making the endometrium less responsive. (5) Embryo transport -- increased ectopic pregnancy risk. (6) Miscarriage -- increased risk from progesterone resistance and inflammation. (7) Ovulation -- severe ovulation defects including Luteinized Unruptured Follicle (LUF) syndrome occur more frequently. Low progesterone is very highly associated with endometriosis and typically does not improve after surgery -- women usually need continued progesterone supplementation. Endometriosis also produces its own nerve fibers and hormones, behaving like a benign cancer.
facts.verification_notes: Promoted from local rrm-cli facts.json (170 curated facts)

facts.claim: Birth control, Lupron have not been shown to stop progression or remove endometriosis
facts.body: Birth control pills, Lupron, and other hormonal suppression medications have NOT been shown to stop the progression of endometriosis or remove the disease. The only demonstrated benefit is possible reduction in likelihood of endometrioma recurrence. There is no actual medical treatment that treats endometriosis itself -- medications only manage symptoms while potentially masking disease progression. Additionally, no RCTs exist comparing NSAIDs vs hormonal contraception for pelvic pain treatment. Suppressive meds given 3+ months prior to surgery can change/suppress the appearance of endo, making it harder to visualize at surgery.
facts.verification_notes: Promoted from local rrm-cli facts.json (170 curated facts)

facts.claim: Endo patients: 32.1% IVF LBR vs 50.7% controls; disease state reduces available embryos
facts.body: Patients with endometriosis have significantly lower cumulative live birth rates from IVF: 32.1% vs. 50.7% for controls. This reduction is attributed to the disease state reducing the number of available embryos (not embryo quality). This is a critical data point: IVF does not address the underlying endo, so the disease environment continues to impair outcomes. RRM's approach of treating the underlying disease before attempting conception addresses this fundamental limitation. High-dose injectable fertility medications (IVF/IUI protocols) may also spread endometriosis -- Dr. Whittaker avoids ovulation meds in patients with endometriomas on imaging and reports that some of her worst endo cases have previously done IVF or IUI.

source.raw_source_id: PMC 7464781; Whittaker NM, RRM vs ART lecture and Endometriosis Masterclass

facts.verification_notes: Promoted from local rrm-cli facts.json (170 curated facts)

facts.claim: Pregnancy does not cure endometriosis; hysterectomy does not cure endometriosis; normal ultrasound does not rule out endometriosis
facts.body: Three commonly perpetuated myths about endometriosis: (1) Pregnancy does NOT cure endometriosis -- this is outdated and not supported by evidence. (2) Hysterectomy does NOT cure endometriosis -- the disease exists outside the uterus. (3) A normal ultrasound does NOT rule out endometriosis -- endo implants are typically only a few millimeters in size, far below ultrasound and MRI detection thresholds. When endometriomas ARE visible on ultrasound, the disease is usually Stage 3 or 4. MRI and colonoscopy are also very limited for endo detection. There is no medical treatment (medication) that treats endometriosis itself -- medications only manage symptoms. No 'chemotherapy-like agent' exists for endo.
facts.verification_notes: Promoted from local rrm-cli facts.json (170 curated facts)

facts.claim: 6-10% general population; 24-35% with pain; 50-60% with infertility
facts.body: Endometriosis prevalence in the general female population is estimated at 6-10%. Prevalence increases to 24-35% in women with pelvic pain and up to 50-60% in women with infertility. In Dr. Whittaker's NaPro practice, over 80% of infertility patients have endometriosis; if they also have pain, estimated over 95%. Bidirectional association: 30-50% of women with endometriosis have infertility, and 25-50% of women with infertility have endometriosis.

source.raw_source_id: Whittaker NM, Endometriosis Masterclass; published epidemiological data

facts.verification_notes: Promoted from local rrm-cli facts.json (170 curated facts)

facts.claim: NAC, Pycnogenol, Magnesium, Ginger have data; Pycnogenol showed pain reduction comparable to Lupron
facts.body: Supplements with published data supporting benefit for endometriosis: (1) N-Acetylcysteine (NAC) -- helps shrink endometriomas (tiny shrinkage visible on ultrasound), improves cervical mucus and egg quality. (2) Pycnogenol (pine tree bark extract) -- showed similar pain reduction compared to Lupron in published data. (3) Magnesium -- smooth muscle relaxation, reduces cramping. (4) Ginger -- pain from endometriosis. RCTs show NSAIDs + ginger and NSAIDs + alpha lipoic acid combinations provide pain benefit. Mefenamic acid (Ponstel) + turmeric also has data. Low-dose naltrexone (LDN) targets inflammation (not pain directly), benefits fertility, and has shown benefit for autoimmune conditions like Crohn's. Most NaPro providers prescribe LDN. Progesterone supplementation is standard for endo patients to offset estrogen dominance. Mediterranean diet is the only diet shown to improve fertility rates. Only proven dietary risk factors for endo: alcohol (increases estrogen) and trans fats (increase adipose estrogen secretion).
facts.verification_notes: Promoted from local rrm-cli facts.json (170 curated facts)

facts.claim: 4 interpretation tiers: 0, 5+ Tier 1, 1+ Tier 1 or 15+ total, all others
facts.body: The survey uses 4 interpretation tiers based on score and Tier 1 count. (1) Score 0: no symptoms reported, recommend discussing any unexplained concerns with a provider. (2) 5+ Tier 1 symptoms: strongest response -- 'You are not crazy.' Acknowledges pattern of being told pain is normal, years on birth control masking symptoms while disease progresses. Recommends thorough investigation for root cause. (3) 1+ Tier 1 symptoms OR total score 15+: 'Your symptoms tell a story worth investigating.' Notes patterns frequently seen in later-diagnosed endo patients. (4) All others (lower scores, no Tier 1): 'Worth keeping on your radar.' Even low scores with quality-of-life impact warrant attention. Key framing: the survey never dismisses any score. Even a score of 1 gets validation. The language is designed to empower patients to advocate for themselves. Output includes a downloadable PDF for the patient to bring to their appointment and a 'Copy Results for AI' button for pasting into AI assistants.
facts.verification_notes: Linked via WS1 citation resolution (2026-04-06)

facts.claim: 3-Tier Endometriosis Symptom Self-Survey: 36 symptoms, 81 points maximum
facts.body: The 3-Tier Endometriosis Symptom Self-Survey was developed by Dr. Naomi Whittaker based on symptom patterns she sees in clinical practice. It uses 36 symptoms across 3 tiers of clinical suspicion, scored on a weighted system with a maximum of 81 points. Tier 1 (Very High Suspicion): 15 symptoms, 3 points each (max 45). Tier 2 (High Suspicion): 15 symptoms, 2 points each (max 30). Tier 3 (Suspicion): 6 symptoms, 1 point each (max 6). The survey is NOT a diagnosis -- endometriosis can only be confirmed through surgery. It is designed to help women assess their level of suspicion, give them language for their symptoms, and help them walk into their next appointment with a clearer picture. Results include a total score, tier breakdown, and personalized interpretation. Available at rrmacademy.org/endo-survey/ (email-gated, magic-link access).
facts.verification_notes: Linked via WS1 citation resolution (2026-04-06)

facts.claim: Tier 1: Very High Suspicion -- 15 symptoms, 3 points each
facts.body: Tier 1 symptoms are the strongest clinical indicators of endometriosis. 15 symptoms, each worth 3 points (max 45). The symptoms are: (1) Vomiting with menstruation. (2) Recurrent tail-end brown bleeding lasting 3+ days. (3) ER visits for pain related to menstruation. (4) Pain during sex (deep penetration) or debilitating pain afterward. (5) Severe pain (8+/10) during or leading up to menstruation. (6) Menses pain interferes with daily activities. (7) Stabbing rectal pain. (8) Pain radiating to back or legs during menstruation. (9) Raw, stabbing, or achy lower abdominal pain with menstruation. (10) Heating pad provides pain relief. (11) Need to curl into fetal position during painful episodes. (12) Severe cramping. (13) Birth control fails to help with pain (or repeated prescriptions of various methods). (14) OTC medications fail to provide relief (or reliance on very high doses). (15) Infertility (especially 'unexplained') accompanied by any of the above symptoms. Interpretation threshold: 5+ Tier 1 symptoms checked triggers the 'You are not crazy' message acknowledging the severity of their experience.
facts.verification_notes: Linked via WS1 citation resolution (2026-04-06)

facts.claim: Tier 2: High Suspicion -- 15 symptoms, 2 points each
facts.body: Tier 2 symptoms are strongly associated with endometriosis but are less specific than Tier 1. 15 symptoms, each worth 2 points (max 30). The symptoms are: (1) Chronic bladder discomfort with urination. (2) Chronic frequent urination. (3) Heavy menstrual bleeding and even iron deficiency. (4) Infertility without any other symptoms. (5) Infertility with history of painful periods at menarche. (6) Low quality or quantity of cervical mucus. (7) Symptoms or diagnosis of IBS (Irritable Bowel Syndrome). (8) Symptoms or diagnosis of IC (Interstitial Cystitis). (9) Severe abdominal bloating, especially during or 1-2 days before menstruation. (10) Migraines. (11) Short menstrual cycles (due to short luteal phases). (12) Severe PMS. (13) Chronic constipation. (14) Consistent 1-2 days of tail-end brown bleeding. (15) Diagnosis of LUF syndrome. Notable: IBS, IC, and migraines are commonly comorbid with endometriosis and often misdiagnosed as the primary condition. Cervical mucus quality is a key FABM/NaPro observation -- low mucus quality in endo patients is documented (Popal 6 Institute data showing decreased mean cervical mucus scores).
facts.verification_notes: Linked via WS1 citation resolution (2026-04-06)

facts.claim: Tier 3: Suspicion -- 6 symptoms, 1 point each
facts.body: Tier 3 symptoms are general indicators that raise suspicion for endometriosis but are the least specific. 6 symptoms, each worth 1 point (max 6). The symptoms are: (1) Fatigue. (2) Nickel sensitivity. (3) Skin reactions or sensitivities. (4) Easy bruising. (5) Family history of endometriosis. (6) Personal history of autoimmune conditions. These reflect the systemic and immune-mediated aspects of endometriosis. Nickel sensitivity and autoimmune associations are emerging research areas. Family history reflects the genetic component (first-degree relatives have ~7x increased risk). These symptoms alone do not indicate endo but in combination with Tier 1 or 2 symptoms they strengthen the clinical picture.
facts.verification_notes: Linked via WS1 citation resolution (2026-04-06)

facts.claim: Symptom severity does NOT correlate with disease stage; Stage 4 seen in patients as young as 16
facts.body: Years of infertility and severity of pain do NOT correlate with the severity (stage) of endometriosis. A patient can have Stage 1 with debilitating pain or Stage 4 with no pain and only infertility. You cannot predict the location of endometriosis from symptoms -- bowel or bladder symptoms don't necessarily mean endo is on that organ, because adjacent organs are irritated by inflammatory fluid. Endometriosis patients are commonly first diagnosed with IBS by gastroenterologists who are not trained to diagnose endo. Stage 4 endo has been seen in patients as young as 16, supporting embryologic origin over retrograde menstruation theory. Endometriosis has been found in unusual locations (brain, thigh, fingernails, males after estrogen treatment) that retrograde menstruation cannot explain.

source.raw_source_id: Whittaker NM, Endometriosis Masterclass; Redwine DB origin theories

facts.claim: 1,077,813 ART cycles across 40 European countries in 2019, producing 203,665 infants (3.0% of births)
facts.body: In 2019 (pre-COVID peak), 40 European countries reported 1,077,813 total ART treatment cycles across 1,488 clinics, resulting in 203,665 ART infants born -- 3.0% of all births in reporting countries. Top 4 by volume: Russia (161,166), Spain (137,276), France (118,394), Germany (107,136). In 2020, COVID reduced cycles to 923,318 (-14.3%) and infants to 182,198 (2.7% of births). The 2019 figure of 1.08 million cycles represents the pre-pandemic scale of European ART. Country-level ART birth share ranged from 0.6% (Spain egg donation skews) to 8.9% (Spain, including cross-border ED).

facts.claim: Sweden 88.8% SET / 2.7% twins vs Germany 34.4% SET / 18.1% twins (ESHRE 2019)
facts.body: In 2019, Sweden transferred single embryos 88.8% of the time vs Germany at 34.4% (65.6% double or triple embryo transfers). Consequences: Sweden fresh twin rate 2.7% vs Germany 18.1%. Germany fresh triplet rate 0.4%. FET twins: Sweden 1.8% vs Germany 13.0%. In 2020, Germany improved to 40.1% SET but still transferred double embryos 57.8% of the time, with a 16.1% fresh twin rate. Germany's regulatory environment (no embryo selection allowed, limited cryopreservation) partially explains the slower SET adoption.

facts.body: European delivery rates per aspiration/thawing for women aged 40+ in 2020: IVF 6.8%, ICSI 5.5%, FET 16.3%. Compare under-34: IVF 21.4%, ICSI 19.5%, FET 29.3%. Ages 35-39: IVF 16.4%, ICSI 14.6%, FET 25.8%. The age-dependent decline is steeper for fresh cycles than for FET. Greece had the highest proportion of women 40+ undergoing IVF (42.5%) and ICSI (47.7%). FET now represents 38.3% of all ART treatments (up from ~25% in 2013). facts.body: HFEA 2018 data on double embryo transfer harm: Under 35, patient eggs + partner sperm: single ET birth rate per ET 32.3%, per cycle 31.7%. The 2018 report states: 'Transferring 2 embryos: 32% multiple birth rate for patients under 35, without increase in live birth rate.' In 2019, under 35: single ET per cycle 38%, double ET per cycle 36.2% -- double ET actually had a LOWER per-cycle rate. In 2018, 79% of under-35 cycles used single ET.

facts.body: Frozen embryo transfer birth rate per embryo transferred overtook fresh around 2014-2015. Timeline: 2010: fresh 23% vs frozen 12% (fresh far ahead). 2013: fresh 19% vs frozen 18% (converging). 2015: fresh ~24% vs frozen 22% per cycle started. 2016: approximate PET parity -- the 2014-2016 HFEA report states 'Frozen PET overtook fresh PET around 2014-2015.' 2017: fresh 23% vs frozen 25%. 2019: fresh 24% vs frozen 27%. 2023: fresh 25% vs frozen 33%. The crossover coincided with widespread vitrification adoption (flash-freezing) replacing slow-freezing in the late 2000s. Frozen cycles grew from ~10% of all IVF in 1991 to 31% in 2016 to 45% in 2023. Fresh cycles declined from 48,370 in 2013 to 42,310 in 2023 while frozen cycles grew from 13,475 to 35,202.

facts.body: Single embryo transfer (SET) adoption in UK IVF: ~10% in early 2000s, 13% in 1991, 22% eSET + 18% non-elective SET in 2012 (40% total), 27% eSET in 2013 (45% total SET), 75% in 2019, 85% in 2022, 88% in 2023. Triple embryo transfers: 61% in 1991, restricted in 2003, down to 1% by 2019. Double embryo transfers: peaked at 56% in 2012, dropped to 24% in 2019, 12% by 2023. The shift to SET drove the rise in frozen embryo transfers (leftover embryos frozen for later use) and the dramatic decline in multiple births. facts.claim: IUI in natural cycles has uncertain benefit vs. expectant management (low quality evidence)
facts.body: Cochrane reviews show IUI has uncertain benefit: 15 trials (2,000 women) found IUI in natural cycles has uncertain benefit vs. expectant management (low quality evidence). 10 RCTs (757 couples) found no evidence of difference in LBR between IUI + ovarian hyperstimulation vs. timed intercourse in natural cycles. Review of 8 studies (932 cycles) found IUI cycle fecundability of 5.6% with Clomiphene alone vs. 8.3% with Clomiphene + IUI (not statistically significant). Despite this weak evidence base, IUI is routinely offered as a first-line fertility treatment.

source.raw_source_id: Cochrane systematic reviews; Dr. Naomi Whittaker IIRRM lecture (citing Spirov textbook)

facts.verification_notes: Promoted from local rrm-cli facts.json (170 curated facts)

facts.claim: 1 live birth per 15 IUI cycles; medication is the benefit, not IUI itself
facts.body: Analysis of IUI data shows the medication (ovulation induction) is the benefit vs. expectant management, not IUI itself. IUI is no better than timed intercourse. When IUI + medication appears to work, it is the medication doing the work. Specific data: 1 live birth per 15 IUI cycles (31% LBR with number needed to treat of 5, each having 3 cycles). Gonadotropins do NOT improve outcomes vs. Clomid with IUI. Additionally, centrifugation of sperm during IUI preparation may cause reactive oxygen species (ROS), potentially impairing sperm function rather than helping it. This challenges the routine prescription of IUI as a first-line treatment.

source.raw_source_id: Whittaker NM, RRM vs ART lecture; Cochrane systematic reviews; Spirov REI textbook

facts.claim: All complications of pregnancy are increased with IVF (ACOG); SMM OR 2.40
facts.body: ACOG committee opinion states all complications of pregnancy are increased with IVF, including: preeclampsia, hypertensive disorders, multiple gestation, preterm birth, preterm labor, C-section, placental abnormalities, ectopic pregnancy, small for gestational age, birth defects, perinatal mortality, low birth weight. Monozygotic twinning is also increased (leading to TTTS and IUGR). OHSS occurs in 5-10% of IVF cycles (up to 30% in some estimates), and 1% of the time it can be life-threatening. Severe Maternal Morbidity (SMM) has an Odds Ratio of 2.40 for fertility treatments vs. spontaneous conception. Even gestational surrogates (proven healthy) carrying IVF-conceived embryos had worse outcomes than their own naturally conceived pregnancies, suggesting the ART process itself affects embryo quality.

source.raw_source_id: ACOG Committee Opinion; Whittaker NM, RRM vs ART lecture; PubMed 29202976

facts.claim: Singleton birth defect RR 1.41; childhood cancer RR 1.42; leukemia OR 2.21; cryo aHR 2.43
facts.body: 2018 meta-analysis: ART children at increased birth defect risk vs. natural conception. Singletons: pooled RR 1.41 (41% increased risk). Another review: adjusted OR 2.0 (100% increased risk) for major congenital malformations. Risk persists in singletons, implicating the ART procedure itself. ICSI specifically increases risk of congenital anomalies, endocrine abnormalities, and epigenetic imprinting effects. Denmark study of 1 million children: IVF embryo transfer associated with increased childhood cancer risk. JAMA: hepatic tumors significantly higher in IVF-conceived children. Moderately increased childhood cancer risk RR 1.42; increased leukemia OR 2.21. Cryopreservation associated with increased cancer risk aHR 2.43. ART singletons also show increased cardiovascular/metabolic risk factors including higher blood pressure.

source.raw_source_id: 2018 meta-analysis; Denmark 1M children study; JAMA; Whittaker NM, RRM vs ART lecture

US national ART data show that cycle starts increased 234.7% from 2012 to 2021 while live births increased only 179.2%; cycle efficiency fell from 29.1% (2012) to 22.2% (2021), a relative reduction of 23.4%, with an approximately linear decline across the decade. facts.claim: Less than 2.5% adhesion scores with full NaPro protocol; Cochrane 18 RCTs, 1,200 women
facts.body: Dr. Hilgers' study demonstrated nearly adhesion-free surgery with NaProTechnology. In Phase 3 (full adhesion prevention protocol), total adhesion scores were less than 2.5%. The NaPro adhesion prevention protocol includes: (1) CO2 laser excision with minimal lateral thermal spread (~1mm vs several mm for cautery). (2) Peritoneal closure -- sewing raw surfaces closed with running imbricating technique (edges turn in, creating smooth surface). Controversial and time-consuming but supported by data. No other specialty routinely does this. (3) Adhesion barriers -- Gore-Tex membrane for extensive disease (sewn in 7-10 days, removed in follow-up procedure), Seprafilm, Interceed. (4) Slurry -- lactated Ringer's + steroid + Seprafilm left in pelvis. (5) Meticulous hemostasis -- blood left in abdomen causes scar tissue. (6) Suture material selection -- Proline (immunologically inert) preferred over Chromic (causes immune flare). Cochrane review: 18 RCTs, 1,200 women confirmed adhesion agents are effective with no adverse events.

source.raw_source_id: Whittaker NM, Endometriosis Masterclass; Hilgers TW adhesion prevention study; Cochrane Database

facts.verification_notes: Promoted from local rrm-cli facts.json (170 curated facts)

facts.claim: 114 laparoscopies (58 robotic), 54 endometriosis surgeries in NaPro fellowship
facts.body: Dr. Whittaker's NaPro fellowship (2018, Pope Paul VI Institute) included: 114 laparoscopies (58 robotic, 54 laparoscopic), 54 endometriosis surgeries, plus training in ovarian wedge resection, selective hysterosalpingogram (sHSG), tubal anastomosis, isthmus seal, and rhinectomy. This contrasts with REI fellowship which has zero minimum cases for endometriosis or tubal surgery, and MIGS fellowship which has no minimum sHSG, tubal surgery, or ovarian wedge resection training. NaPro fellowship fills the training void between REI (fertility focus, minimal surgery) and MIGS (surgical focus, minimal fertility), training surgeons in both comprehensive reproductive surgery AND fertility-specific procedures.

source.raw_source_id: Whittaker NM, RRM vs ART lecture

facts.verification_notes: Promoted from local rrm-cli facts.json (170 curated facts)

facts.claim: Treat endometriosis like a cancer; ~3 dozen members in Society of Procreative Surgeons
facts.body: NaPro treats endometriosis like a cancer -- meticulous excision including microscopic disease, while preserving and enhancing fertility. This combines MIGS surgical aggressiveness with REI fertility focus, filling a void that neither specialty alone addresses. The Society of Procreative Surgeons (SPS) has approximately 3 dozen members. For complex cases, NaPro uses a staged approach: (1) Diagnostic laparoscopy to find answers and treat mild disease, (2) Robotic surgery with subspecialists for advanced disease if needed. All excision ideally in one session with full adhesion prevention. Key principle: no surgery is better than a non-optimal surgery -- a suboptimal first surgery creates post-surgical scarring indistinguishable from endometriosis scarring. At 18 months post-NaPro surgery, if still not conceiving, a second-look surgery is considered.
facts.verification_notes: Promoted from local rrm-cli facts.json (170 curated facts)

facts.claim: Only 6.5% truly normal pelvis; 89% had endometriosis on re-evaluation with NCL
facts.body: When patients with a previously 'normal' laparoscopy by another surgeon were re-examined by a NaPro specialist using near-contact laparoscopy, only 6.5% had a truly normal pelvis and 89% had endometriosis. This demonstrates that standard laparoscopy routinely misses endometriosis. The missed disease is often subtle: red/clear vesicles (more common in teenagers, more painful, most likely to be missed), white scars, halo lesions, peritoneal pockets (Allen-Masters Windows that can be deep tunnels), and hemocitrine (brown staining). Black 'classic' lesions are the easiest to see but represent trapped old blood and are less common in younger patients.

source.raw_source_id: Whittaker NM, Endometriosis Masterclass; NaPro clinical data

facts.verification_notes: Promoted from local rrm-cli facts.json (170 curated facts)

facts.claim: Stein and Leventhal discovered ovarian wedge resection incidentally during 1930s PCOS research
facts.body: The ovarian wedge resection technique was incidentally discovered in the 1930s by Stein and Leventhal when they were performing ovarian biopsies during PCOS research. They observed that patients who had undergone biopsy subsequently ovulated and conceived. Modern NaPro/RRM training revived and refined this approach as part of the PEARS (Procedures, Evaluation, and Restorative Surgery) protocol. The technique involves removing a wedge of ovarian tissue to reduce androgen-producing stroma. It is distinct from ovarian drilling (which RRM content should never endorse -- see guardrail-no-ovarian-drilling).

source.title: Amenorrhea Associated with Bilateral Polycystic Ovaries
source.journal: American journal of obstetrics and gynecology
source.authors: Stein IF, Leventhal ML

facts.body: Figure 14 trend chart shows the dramatic rise in SET from ~10% (2000) to ~75% (2013). facts.claim: In Sweden, the introduction of single embryo transfer (SET) as standard practice dramatically reduced the IVF multiple birth rate from approximately 25% in 2000 to approximately 5% by 2013, while delivery rates remained stable at 25-27% per fresh cycle (Q-IVF Annual Report 2015, Figure 14, page 21)
facts.body: Figure 14 shows three trend lines: SET rate rose from ~10% (2000) to ~75% (2013), multiple birth rate fell from ~25% (2000) to ~5% (2013), and delivery rate remained flat at 25-27%. This demonstrates that mandatory SET policy achieved its safety goal without sacrificing effectiveness. The sharpest changes occurred 2001-2004 following the 2003 National Board of Health and Welfare regulation mandating SET as routine.

facts.body: Based on 4,341 IVF babies out of all births in Sweden in 2014. IVF has been clinically established in Sweden for over 20 years with approximately 19,000 treatments per year at this time.

facts.claim: Sweden reduced its IVF multiple birth rate for fresh embryos from over 30% to below 5% over a 25-year period through adoption of single embryo transfer, the lowest rate in the world (Q-IVF Annual Report 2016, Summary, page 13)
facts.body: The 2016 report marked the first year the multiple birth rate dropped below 5% (4.7% fresh). This decline is attributed to Sweden leading the world in SET adoption, which reached 80% for fresh cycles in 2014. Twins are the main component; triplet rates are near zero.

facts.claim: The total multiple birth rate after fresh IVF cycles in Sweden was 4.7% in 2014, dropping below 5% for the first time in the registry history (Q-IVF Annual Report 2016, comment below Figure 10, page 13)
facts.body: This represents a decline from over 30% to under 5% over a 25-year period for fresh embryos, driven by Sweden leading the world in single embryo transfer adoption. The rate is somewhat higher in older age groups because they more frequently receive two embryos.

facts.body: Figure 26, page 36. 4th IVF-KUPP survey. Five domains: Medical care 91%, Accessibility 83%, Information 85%, Responsiveness 92%, Participation 69%. n=2,900 responses. Women response rate 70.7%, partners 49.5%. All five domains stable or improving vs previous surveys (2013-2016).

facts.body: Sum of all treatment types from Table 2, page 11. Fresh IVF/ICSI and freeze cycle counts include IVF with donated gametes (egg/sperm). Concluding remarks confirm 'more than 21,000 treatments'. 17 clinics (6 public, 11 private) with 100% registry participation.

facts.claim: Of 12,810 fresh IVF/ICSI cycles started in Sweden in 2016 (all gamete types), 11,851 reached oocyte retrieval, 9,754 reached embryo transfer, 3,374 achieved clinical pregnancy, and 2,809 resulted in delivery (Q-IVF Annual Report 2018, Figure 4)
facts.body: Figure 4, page 12. Treatment attrition: 7.5% did not reach OPU, 17.7% of OPUs did not reach ET, 65.4% of ETs did not result in clinical pregnancy, 16.7% of clinical pregnancies did not result in delivery. Overall: 21.9% of started cycles resulted in delivery.

facts.body: For donated oocytes, the recipient woman's age does not significantly affect outcomes; the donor's age is more important. Donors are mostly younger women. facts.claim: In Sweden in 2018, for the first time freeze cycle live births (2,524) equaled fresh IVF live births (2,522) across all treatment types including donated gametes (Q-IVF Annual Report 2020, Figure 19).
facts.body: A landmark shift: freeze cycles matched fresh IVF in total live births for the first time in 2018. This trend reflects improved vitrification methods, extended culture to blastocyst stage, and growing freeze-all strategies. Figure 19 includes all treatments (own gametes + donated gametes + PGT).

facts.body: OHSS is the most common IVF complication. Moderate or severe forms occur in 1-5% of women. The 0.38% rate represents only severe cases requiring hospitalization.
