facts.claim: Well-differentiated papillary mesothelioma (WDPM) of the peritoneum carries distinct mutations in EHD1, ATM, FBXO10, SH2D2A, CDH5, MAGED1, and TP73 that are not reported in malignant mesothelioma.

source.title: Well-Differentiated Papillary Mesothelioma of the Peritoneum Is Genetically Distinct from Malignant Mesothelioma
source.authors: Shrestha R, Nabavi N, Volik S, Anderson S, Haegert A, McConeghy B, Sar F, Brahmbhatt S, Bell R, Le Bihan S, Wang Y, Collins C, Churg A

facts.verification_notes: Quote: 'Using whole exome sequencing of five WDPMs of the peritoneum, we have identified distinct mutations in EHD1, ATM, FBXO10, SH2D2A, CDH5, MAGED1, and TP73 shared by WDPM cases but not reported in malignant mesotheliomas.' Source: Shrestha R et al., Cancers (Basel) 2020;12(6):1568. PMID 32545767; DOI 10.3390/cancers12061568.

facts.claim: WDPM is an uncommon mesothelial proliferation that is most commonly encountered as an incidental finding in the peritoneal cavity; it is genetically distinct from malignant mesothelioma.

source.title: Well-Differentiated Papillary Mesothelioma of the Peritoneum Is Genetically Distinct from Malignant Mesothelioma

And study title: 'Well-Differentiated Papillary Mesothelioma of the Peritoneum Is Genetically Distinct from Malignant Mesothelioma.' Source: Shrestha R et al., Cancers 2020, PMID 32545767.

facts.claim: WDPM is strongly enriched with C>A transversion substitution mutations — a pattern not found in malignant mesothelioma.

facts.claim: In the EAGeR Trial cohort of 1,228 women with 1-2 prior pregnancy losses, each log-unit (μg/dL) increase in preconception serum α-carotene was associated with shorter time to pregnancy (adjusted FOR = 1.17, 95% CI: 1.00, 1.36).

facts.claim: In the ENDO Study operative cohort, the incidence of surgically visualized endometriosis was 40% (190/473), with 11.8% moderate/severe and 28.3% minimal/mild disease.

source.title: Risk factors associated with endometriosis: importance of study population for characterizing disease in the ENDO Study
source.journal: American journal of obstetrics and gynecology
source.authors: Peterson CM, Johnstone EB, Hammoud AO, Stanford JB, Varner MW, Kennedy A, Chen Z, Sun L, Fujimoto VY, Hediger ML, Buck Louis GM, ENDO Study Working Group

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "incidence of surgically visualized endometriosis in the operative cohort was 40% (190/473 with 11.8% [56/473] moderate/severe" Section: Results.

facts.claim: The reported prevalence of endometriosis varies widely by study population: 0.7-11% in healthcare-seeking women, 2-22% in surgical sterilization patients, 17-47% in infertile women, and 2-74% in women with chronic pelvic pain.

source.title: Risk factors associated with endometriosis: importance of study population for characterizing disease in the ENDO Study
source.journal: American journal of obstetrics and gynecology
source.authors: Peterson CM, Johnstone EB, Hammoud AO, Stanford JB, Varner MW, Kennedy A, Chen Z, Sun L, Fujimoto VY, Hediger ML, Buck Louis GM, ENDO Study Working Group

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "0.7–11% in populations presenting for health care, 2–22% when undergoing surgical sterilization, 17–47% among infertile women, and 2–74%" Section: Introduction.

facts.claim: When restricting to stage 3-4 endometriosis (11.8%, 56/473 of operative cohort), parity remained strongly protective (AOR 0.19; 95% CI 0.10-0.37) and age at first sex predictive (AOR 1.11; 95% CI 1.03-1.19).

source.title: Risk factors associated with endometriosis: importance of study population for characterizing disease in the ENDO Study

facts.claim: More than 80% of women in all study groups had a history of oral contraceptive use, which may have masked menstrual cycle differences between women with and without endometriosis.

facts.claim: In this cohort study, 24.0% of women with HIV (76/317) had ever experienced prolonged secondary amenorrhea compared with 13.3% of women without HIV (56/420).

facts.claim: The study analyzed 483 mother-child dyads across 5 ECHO cohorts (MARCH, RESONANCE, VDAART, Rochester, WISC) using 16S rRNA gene amplicon sequencing.

source.title: Maternal vaginal and fecal microbiota in later pregnancy contribute to child fecal microbiota development in the ECHO cohort

source.title: Maternal vaginal and fecal microbiota in later pregnancy contribute to child fecal microbiota development in the ECHO cohort

source.title: Maternal vaginal and fecal microbiota in later pregnancy contribute to child fecal microbiota development in the ECHO cohort

facts.claim: Among 483 mother-child dyads, 73.7% of children were born vaginally, 52.0% of mothers did not take prenatal antibiotics, and mean maternal age at delivery was 30.1 (SD 4.9) years.

source.title: Maternal vaginal and fecal microbiota in later pregnancy contribute to child fecal microbiota development in the ECHO cohort

source.title: Maternal vaginal and fecal microbiota in later pregnancy contribute to child fecal microbiota development in the ECHO cohort
source.authors: Liu T, Kress AM, Debelius J, Zhao N, Smirnova E, Bandyopadhyay S, Bonham K, Comstock SS, Gill S, Gern JE, Koinis-Mitchell D, Klepac-Ceraj V, Lee-Sarwar K, Litonjua AA, McKee K, McCauley K, O'Connor TG, Rosas-Salazar C, Scheible K, Stanford JB, Moore B, Jacobson LP, Mueller NT, program collaborators for Environmental Influences on Child Health Outcomes

facts.claim: In the RESONANCE cohort, shared maternal-child fecal ASVs increased with child age: ~25% at 2 months, 30% at 5 months, 35% at 13 months, and 45% at 32 months (p<0.001).

source.title: Maternal vaginal and fecal microbiota in later pregnancy contribute to child fecal microbiota development in the ECHO cohort

facts.claim: Maternal pregnancy vaginal microbiota contributions to child fecal microbiota decreased with age: in WISC, ASV sharing declined from 25% at 2 months to ~20% at 9 months (p<0.001); in Rochester, from 15% at 1 month to 10% at 12 months (p<0.01).

source.title: The association between preconception polycystic ovary syndrome and gestational diabetes mellitus among women with and without pre-pregnancy hypertension: a cross-sectional study from Utah's Pregnancy Risk Assessment Monitoring System Survey (2016-2021)

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "1.53 times (95% CI: 1.16–2.01) times as likely to have GDM using the PRAMS survey measure" Section: Results.

facts.claim: Women with PCOS had significantly higher rates of obesity (42.8% vs. 21.2%) compared to women without PCOS in the Utah PRAMS sample.

source.authors: Myrer RS, Adediran E, Ellsworth AD, Ceballos RM, Lopez I, Stanford JB, Talboys S, Wang J, Schliep KC

facts.claim: Women with PCOS had significantly higher rates of depression (35.9% vs. 17.1%) and anxiety (48.8% vs. 26.4%) compared to women without PCOS.

facts.claim: The Yuzpe regimen consists of high-dose ethinyl estradiol combined with high-dose levonorgestrel taken within a 12-hour interval, and remains the most prevalent form of hormonal emergency contraception in the US and Europe.

source.title: Postfertilization effect of hormonal emergency contraception
source.authors: Kahlenborn C, Larimore WL, Stanford JB

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "the Yuzpe regimen remains the most prevalent form of EC in the US and Europe." Section: Introduction.

facts.claim: Trussell and Raymond estimated that even assuming hormonal EC prevents ovulation in 87.5% of treated women, mechanisms other than preventing ovulation account for 13-38% of the estimated effectiveness of the Yuzpe regimen.

source.title: Postfertilization effect of hormonal emergency contraception
source.authors: Kahlenborn C, Larimore WL, Stanford JB

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "a mechanism 'other than preventing ovulation ac- counts for 13–38% of the estimated effectiveness of the Yuzpe regimen.'" Section: Relative Contribution.

facts.claim: The current background proportion of clinical pregnancies that are ectopic is approximately 2%, providing a baseline against which post-EC ectopic rates can be compared.

source.title: Postfertilization effect of hormonal emergency contraception
source.authors: Kahlenborn C, Larimore WL, Stanford JB

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The current proportion of clinical pregnancies that are ec- topic is a little less than 2%." Section: Increased Risk of Ectopic Pregnancy?

facts.claim: Trussell's recalculated overall efficacy rate for the Yuzpe regimen across 8 trials is 74.1%, while raw data from the same trials yields only 65.7%, indicating efficacy estimates are highly tentative.

source.title: Postfertilization effect of hormonal emergency contraception
source.authors: Kahlenborn C, Larimore WL, Stanford JB

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "calculate an overall efficacy rate of 74.1%, while the raw data yield a figure of 65.7%." Section: Appendix I.

facts.claim: Hormonal EC efficacy estimates rely on historical controls rather than randomized prospective trials; FDA approval was granted without evidence from a randomized controlled prospective study of effectiveness.

source.title: Postfertilization effect of hormonal emergency contraception
source.authors: Kahlenborn C, Larimore WL, Stanford JB

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Hormonal EC use received Food and Drug Administration approval without evidence of a randomized, controlled, prospective study" Section: Appendix I.

facts.claim: In a Yuzpe regimen study using serum hormonal markers, an LH peak occurred within 4 days after treatment in 5 of 9 women with subsequent progesterone rise, indicating ovulation was not consistently suppressed.

source.title: Postfertilization effect of hormonal emergency contraception
source.authors: Kahlenborn C, Larimore WL, Stanford JB

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "an LH peak concentration occurred within 4 days after the treatment in 5 of 9 women" Section: Effects on Ovulation.

facts.claim: In a urine hormonal marker trial of the Yuzpe regimen, an LH peak occurred within 1 day of treatment in 2 of 8 women with confirmatory progesterone rise, demonstrating ovulation occurred despite EC use.

source.title: Postfertilization effect of hormonal emergency contraception
source.authors: Kahlenborn C, Larimore WL, Stanford JB

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "found an LH peak concentration within 1 day of treatment with the Yuzpe regimen in 2 of 8 women" Section: Effects on Ovulation.

facts.claim: Reported efficacy rates for the Yuzpe regimen across major studies range from 56.9% to 90.9%, with the largest WHO trial showing only 56.9% efficacy.

source.title: Postfertilization effect of hormonal emergency contraception
source.authors: Kahlenborn C, Larimore WL, Stanford JB

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The estimated efficacy rates range from 56.9% to 90.9%, with the largest trial showing a 56.9% efficacy rate." Section: Effects in Preovulatory Phase.

facts.claim: In the WHO trial, 6 of approximately 400 women using the Yuzpe regimen in the preovulatory phase became pregnant (10 expected), and 2 of those using Plan B preovulatorily became pregnant (11 expected).

source.title: Postfertilization effect of hormonal emergency contraception
source.authors: Kahlenborn C, Larimore WL, Stanford JB

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "6 who used the Yuzpe regimen in the preovulatory phase became pregnant (10 were expected" Section: Effects in Preovulatory Phase.

facts.claim: In the WHO study, Yuzpe regimen used in the ovulatory phase resulted in 17 pregnancies (54 expected) and Plan B resulted in 7 pregnancies (53 expected), consistent with a postfertilization effect on the endometrium.

source.title: Postfertilization effect of hormonal emergency contraception
source.authors: Kahlenborn C, Larimore WL, Stanford JB

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "17 pregnancies oc- curred (54 were expected if EC was not used)" Section: Effects on Implantation.

facts.claim: Oral contraceptives lower uterine prostaglandin concentrations and decrease endometrial thickness as verified by MRI, supporting that hormonal EC similarly impairs uterine receptivity for implantation.

source.title: Postfertilization effect of hormonal emergency contraception
source.authors: Kahlenborn C, Larimore WL, Stanford JB

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "OC use decreases the thickness of the endometrium as verified by magnetic resonance imaging scans" Section: Effects on Implantation.

facts.claim: Oral contraceptives alter epithelial and stromal integrin expression in the endometrium, suggesting impaired uterine receptivity is one mechanism whereby OCs and hormonal EC exert contraceptive action.

source.title: Postfertilization effect of hormonal emergency contraception
source.authors: Kahlenborn C, Larimore WL, Stanford JB

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "impaired uterine receptivity is one mechanism whereby OCs exert their contraceptive action." Section: Effects on Implantation.

facts.claim: In a series of 715 women using the Yuzpe regimen reported by Kubba and Guillebaud, 1 of 17 resulting pregnancies was ectopic, yielding a 5.9% ectopic rate compared to the background rate of less than 2%.

source.title: Postfertilization effect of hormonal emergency contraception
source.authors: Kahlenborn C, Larimore WL, Stanford JB

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "in 715 women who used the Yuzpe regimen, 17 pregnancies occurred, including 1 ectopic pregnancy (i.e., a 5.9% rate" Section: Increased Risk of Ectopic Pregnancy?

facts.claim: In more than 30,000 cycles of modern natural family planning users studied at the Duesseldorf research center, no pregnancy occurred when intercourse took place during the luteal phase as diagnosed by elevated basal body temperature and cervical mucus evaluation.

source.title: More than one fertile ovulation per cycle?
source.authors: Frank-Herrmann P, Freundl G, Stanford JB, Mikolajczyk RT

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Among more than 30,000 cycles in users of modern natural family planning methods studied at the research center in Duesseldorf, no pregnancy occurred" Section: Letter to Editor.

facts.claim: For anecdotal late pregnancies during calendar method use, delayed ovulation (which is relatively frequent) is a more plausible explanation than a second ovulation within the same cycle.

source.title: More than one fertile ovulation per cycle?
source.authors: Frank-Herrmann P, Freundl G, Stanford JB, Mikolajczyk RT

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "For anecdotal evidence of unplanned 'late' pregnancies during the use of the calendar method, delayed ovulation is a much more plausible explanation" Section: Letter to Editor.

facts.claim: Symptothermal method effectiveness studies show that when intercourse occurs outside the fertile window (determined by basal body temperature and cervical mucus signs), the probability of pregnancy is far less than 0.1% per cycle.

source.title: More than one fertile ovulation per cycle?
source.authors: Frank-Herrmann P, Freundl G, Stanford JB, Mikolajczyk RT

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "when intercourse occurs outside the fertile window (as determined by the above signs), the probability of pregnancy is far less than 0.1% per cycle" Section: Letter to Editor.

facts.claim: The Creighton Model System defines the fertile window as 6 days before and 4 days after the mucus peak day, with the peak day serving as a clinical marker of ovulation.

source.title: More than one fertile ovulation per cycle?
source.authors: Frank-Herrmann P, Freundl G, Stanford JB, Mikolajczyk RT

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "a fertile window of 6 days before and 4 days after the mucus peak day, a clinical marker of ovulation" Section: Letter to Editor.

facts.claim: In the Baerwald et al. ultrasonographic study of 63 patients, 50 had normal biphasic cycles; of those, 34 exhibited two waves of follicular development and 16 had three waves, but the last wave always produced the dominant ovulatory follicle.

source.title: More than one fertile ovulation per cycle?
source.authors: Frank-Herrmann P, Freundl G, Stanford JB, Mikolajczyk RT

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "34 exhibited two waves of follicular development and 16 patients had three waves of follicular diameter. However, it was always the last wave" Section: Letter to Editor.

facts.claim: In the Baerwald cohort, 13 of 63 patients were excluded because their cycle duration deviations were considered outside the range of normal ovulation, leaving 50 patients with normal biphasic cycles for analysis.

source.title: More than one fertile ovulation per cycle?
source.authors: Frank-Herrmann P, Freundl G, Stanford JB, Mikolajczyk RT

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Of the 63 participants, 50 had normal biphasic cycles and could thus be included. Thirteen patients were excluded" Section: Letter to Editor.

facts.claim: Follicular development during the luteal phase is regularly observed by women using the symptothermal method, who typically note one day of cervical fluid increase and a short decrease in basal body temperature during this time.

source.title: More than one fertile ovulation per cycle?
source.authors: Frank-Herrmann P, Freundl G, Stanford JB, Mikolajczyk RT

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "they usually note 1 day of cervical fluid increase and a short decrease in basal body temperature" Section: Wettstein letter.

facts.claim: In a retrospective analysis of 1,165 Canadian women living with HIV, 61% of pregnancies were unintended, exceeding the Canadian average of up to 40%.

source.title: Factors associated with contraceptive use among women living with HIV in Canada:  a controlled, cross-sectional study
source.authors: Chadni C Khondoker, Angela Kaida, Anna Marquez, Amber R Campbell, Hélène C F Côté, Arianne Y Albert, Neora Pick, Evelyn J Maan, Emilie A B Russell, Jerilynn C Prior, Chelsea Elwood, Jason Brophy, Melanie C M Murray \r\n

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "1165 Canadian WLWH reported that 61% of pregnancies were unintended [2]; a value that exceeds the Canadian average of up to 40%" Section: Background.

facts.claim: Overall contraceptive use prevalence after age adjustment was 80% in women living with HIV vs. 63% in controls (p=0.06).

source.title: Factors associated with contraceptive use among women living with HIV in Canada:  a controlled, cross-sectional study
source.authors: Chadni C Khondoker, Angela Kaida, Anna Marquez, Amber R Campbell, Hélène C F Côté, Arianne Y Albert, Neora Pick, Evelyn J Maan, Emilie A B Russell, Jerilynn C Prior, Chelsea Elwood, Jason Brophy, Melanie C M Murray \r\n

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Overall prevalence of contraceptive use was similar between WLWH and controls after adjusting for age (80% vs. 63%; p = 0.06)" Section: Contraception.

facts.claim: Approximately 9-21% of sexually active women living with HIV in Canada use hormonal contraceptives, less than half the rate of the general Canadian population (44%).

source.title: Factors associated with contraceptive use among women living with HIV in Canada:  a controlled, cross-sectional study
source.authors: Chadni C Khondoker, Angela Kaida, Anna Marquez, Amber R Campbell, Hélène C F Côté, Arianne Y Albert, Neora Pick, Evelyn J Maan, Emilie A B Russell, Jerilynn C Prior, Chelsea Elwood, Jason Brophy, Melanie C M Murray \r\n

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "9–21% of sexually active WLWH in Canada use hormonal contraceptives... a rate less than half of that used by the general Canadian population (44%)" Section: Background.

facts.claim: Antiretrovirals efavirenz, darunavir, and combined lopinavir/ritonavir are contraindicated with combined hormonal contraceptives; elvitegravir or atazanavir require higher-dose ethinyl estradiol.

source.title: Factors associated with contraceptive use among women living with HIV in Canada:  a controlled, cross-sectional study
source.authors: Chadni C Khondoker, Angela Kaida, Anna Marquez, Amber R Campbell, Hélène C F Côté, Arianne Y Albert, Neora Pick, Evelyn J Maan, Emilie A B Russell, Jerilynn C Prior, Chelsea Elwood, Jason Brophy, Melanie C M Murray \r\n

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Use of efavirenz, darunavir, or combined use of lopinavir/ritonavir in tandem with CHC is contraindicated" Section: Background.

facts.claim: The CARMA-ENDO cross-sectional analysis included 145 premenopausal sexually active women aged 16-49: 83 women living with HIV and 62 HIV-negative controls.

source.title: Factors associated with contraceptive use among women living with HIV in Canada:  a controlled, cross-sectional study
source.authors: Chadni C Khondoker, Angela Kaida, Anna Marquez, Amber R Campbell, Hélène C F Côté, Arianne Y Albert, Neora Pick, Evelyn J Maan, Emilie A B Russell, Jerilynn C Prior, Chelsea Elwood, Jason Brophy, Melanie C M Murray \r\n

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "We analyzed data from 145 premenopausal sexually active women that included 83 WLWH and 62 controls between 16–49 years of age" Section: Demographics.

facts.claim: Among women living with HIV, 92% were on antiretrovirals with mean CD4 count 541 (±337) cells/mm3, and 75% had undetectable viral load (<40 copies/mL).

source.title: Factors associated with contraceptive use among women living with HIV in Canada:  a controlled, cross-sectional study
source.authors: Chadni C Khondoker, Angela Kaida, Anna Marquez, Amber R Campbell, Hélène C F Côté, Arianne Y Albert, Neora Pick, Evelyn J Maan, Emilie A B Russell, Jerilynn C Prior, Chelsea Elwood, Jason Brophy, Melanie C M Murray \r\n

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "92% were on ARVs, and mean (± SD) CD4 count was 541 (± 337) cells/mm3. Of those women on ARVs, 75% had an undetectable HIV viral load" Section: Demographics.

facts.claim: 54% of women living with HIV had at least one contraindication to combined hormonal contraceptive use compared to 13% of controls (p=0.0001).

source.title: Factors associated with contraceptive use among women living with HIV in Canada:  a controlled, cross-sectional study
source.authors: Chadni C Khondoker, Angela Kaida, Anna Marquez, Amber R Campbell, Hélène C F Côté, Arianne Y Albert, Neora Pick, Evelyn J Maan, Emilie A B Russell, Jerilynn C Prior, Chelsea Elwood, Jason Brophy, Melanie C M Murray \r\n

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "54% of WLWH had at least one contraindication to CHC use compared to 13% of controls (p = 0.0001)" Section: Contraindications.

facts.claim: Drug contraindications to CHC among women living with HIV resulted from ARV use: efavirenz (10), darunavir (9), and lopinavir/ritonavir (2).

source.title: Factors associated with contraceptive use among women living with HIV in Canada:  a controlled, cross-sectional study
source.authors: Chadni C Khondoker, Angela Kaida, Anna Marquez, Amber R Campbell, Hélène C F Côté, Arianne Y Albert, Neora Pick, Evelyn J Maan, Emilie A B Russell, Jerilynn C Prior, Chelsea Elwood, Jason Brophy, Melanie C M Murray \r\n

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "All noted drug contraindications for WLWH resulted from ARV medication use: efavirenz (10), darunavir (9) and lopinavir/ritonavir (2)" Section: Contraindications.

facts.claim: Adjusted for age, women living with HIV were 82% less likely to use combined hormonal contraceptives than controls (AOR 0.18, 95%CI 0.04-0.63; p=0.006).

source.title: Factors associated with contraceptive use among women living with HIV in Canada:  a controlled, cross-sectional study
source.authors: Chadni C Khondoker, Angela Kaida, Anna Marquez, Amber R Campbell, Hélène C F Côté, Arianne Y Albert, Neora Pick, Evelyn J Maan, Emilie A B Russell, Jerilynn C Prior, Chelsea Elwood, Jason Brophy, Melanie C M Murray \r\n

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "WLWH were 82% less likely to use CHC (AOR 0.18 (95%CI 0.04–0.63); p-0.006) compared with controls" Section: Contraception.

facts.claim: In the MOS2 pilot, 8 women with physician-diagnosed PCOS were matched 1:3 with 24 controls by age (within 0.6 years) and BMI (within 0.19 kg/m2).

source.title: Experiences of women living with Polycystic Ovary Syndrome: A pilot case-control,  single-cycle, daily Menstrual Cycle Diary study during the SARS-CoV-2 pandemic
source.authors: Nelson K, Shirin S, Kalidasan D, Prior JC

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Eight reported physician-diagnosed PCOS and were matched (1:3 ratio) with controls by age (within .6 years) and BMI (within .19 BMI units)." Section: Abstract.

facts.claim: Increasing age within premenopausal years is associated with improved menstrual cycle regularity and ovulation in women with PCOS (Brown 2011, Welt 2013, Elting 2000).

source.title: Experiences of women living with Polycystic Ovary Syndrome: A pilot case-control,  single-cycle, daily Menstrual Cycle Diary study during the SARS-CoV-2 pandemic
source.authors: Nelson K, Shirin S, Kalidasan D, Prior JC

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "increased ages within the premenopausal years are associated with improved normality of reproduction in women with PCOS" Section: Discussion.

facts.claim: In the MOS2 PCOS subset, 37.5% were obese (BMI >=30) and 25% overweight; ethnicity was 62.5% White, 25% East Asian, 12.5% other/mixed; none currently smoked.

source.title: Experiences of women living with Polycystic Ovary Syndrome: A pilot case-control,  single-cycle, daily Menstrual Cycle Diary study during the SARS-CoV-2 pandemic
source.authors: Nelson K, Shirin S, Kalidasan D, Prior JC

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Obese (>= 30) 3 (37.5)... White 5 (62.5)... East Asian (e.g. Chinese, Korean) 2 (25.0)" Section: Results / Table 1.

facts.claim: Outside Stresses scores (1-5 scale) were significantly higher in PCOS participants (3.4 +/- 0.2) than in matched controls (3.0 +/- 0.5), P = 0.020.

source.title: Experiences of women living with Polycystic Ovary Syndrome: A pilot case-control,  single-cycle, daily Menstrual Cycle Diary study during the SARS-CoV-2 pandemic
source.authors: Nelson K, Shirin S, Kalidasan D, Prior JC

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Outside Stresses were significantly higher in women diagnosed with PCOS compared with controls (P = .020)" Section: Results / Table 3.

facts.claim: Contrary to hypothesis, Diary self-worth, anxiety, and depression scores did not differ significantly between PCOS cases and BMI/age-matched controls.

source.title: Experiences of women living with Polycystic Ovary Syndrome: A pilot case-control,  single-cycle, daily Menstrual Cycle Diary study during the SARS-CoV-2 pandemic
source.authors: Nelson K, Shirin S, Kalidasan D, Prior JC

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "there were no significant differences in feelings of self-worth, anxiety nor depression." Section: Abstract.

facts.claim: Anovulation occurred in 5 of 7 PCOS participants and 9 of 24 controls; luteal phase lengths did not differ significantly between groups (P = 0.167).

source.title: Experiences of women living with Polycystic Ovary Syndrome: A pilot case-control,  single-cycle, daily Menstrual Cycle Diary study during the SARS-CoV-2 pandemic
source.authors: Nelson K, Shirin S, Kalidasan D, Prior JC

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "luteal phase lengths were also similar between PCOS and controls (P = .167); anovulation occurred in 5 with PCOS, and in 9 controls." Section: Abstract.

facts.claim: Mean menstrual cycle length was 38.8 +/- 29.4 days in PCOS cases versus 30.2 +/- 3.1 days in controls (P = 0.593, n.s.); cycles ranged 21-111 days in PCOS and 24-38 days in controls.

source.title: Experiences of women living with Polycystic Ovary Syndrome: A pilot case-control,  single-cycle, daily Menstrual Cycle Diary study during the SARS-CoV-2 pandemic
source.authors: Nelson K, Shirin S, Kalidasan D, Prior JC

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Cycle lengths in women with PCOS ranged from 21 to 111 days; in the controls cycle lengths were from 24 to 38 days." Section: Results / Table 2.

facts.claim: Subclinical ovulatory disturbances (short luteal phase or anovulation) were highly prevalent in both groups: 85.7% of PCOS cases and 66.7% of controls.

source.title: Experiences of women living with Polycystic Ovary Syndrome: A pilot case-control,  single-cycle, daily Menstrual Cycle Diary study during the SARS-CoV-2 pandemic
source.authors: Nelson K, Shirin S, Kalidasan D, Prior JC

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "the presence of subclinical ovulatory disturbances, including short luteal phases anovulation, were high in both cases (85.7%) and controls (66.7%)." Section: Results.

facts.claim: Across the entire MOS2 cohort (n=108), 63% of community-dwelling participants experienced ovulatory disturbances during the SARS-CoV-2 pandemic.

source.title: Experiences of women living with Polycystic Ovary Syndrome: A pilot case-control,  single-cycle, daily Menstrual Cycle Diary study during the SARS-CoV-2 pandemic
source.authors: Nelson K, Shirin S, Kalidasan D, Prior JC

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "in the overall MOS2 data, we found that 63% of all participants (n = 108) experienced ovulatory disturbances" Section: Discussion.

facts.claim: Ovulation was confirmed using the validated Quantitative Basal Temperature (QBT) method; normal luteal phases average ~12 days, short luteal phases show <10 days of elevated temperature, and anovulation is diagnosed when no significant rise occurs or rise lasts <4 days.

source.title: Experiences of women living with Polycystic Ovary Syndrome: A pilot case-control,  single-cycle, daily Menstrual Cycle Diary study during the SARS-CoV-2 pandemic
source.authors: Nelson K, Shirin S, Kalidasan D, Prior JC

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Using QBT, normal luteal phase lengths average ~12 days but are at least 10 days long." Section: Methods / Data collection tools.

facts.claim: PCOS prevalence in population-based cohorts of menstruating individuals is approximately 1 in 10 (Bozdag 2016 meta-analysis).

source.title: Experiences of women living with Polycystic Ovary Syndrome: A pilot case-control,  single-cycle, daily Menstrual Cycle Diary study during the SARS-CoV-2 pandemic
source.authors: Nelson K, Shirin S, Kalidasan D, Prior JC

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) with androgen excess affects 1:10 in population-based cohorts of menstruating individuals" Section: Introduction.

facts.claim: In the ENDO Study operative cohort, women with visualised endometriosis had a higher prevalence of miscarriages with adjusted prevalence ratio 1.48 (95% CI 1.23, 1.77) compared with women without endometriosis.

source.title: Endometriosis diagnosis, staging and typology and adverse pregnancy outcome  history
source.authors: Schliep KC, Farland LV, Pollack AZ, Buck Louis G, Stanford JB, Allen-Brady K, Varner MW, Kah K, Peterson CM

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "a higher prevalence of miscarriages, aPR 1.48 (95% CI 1.23, 1.77) compared with women without endometriosis" Section: Results.

facts.claim: Endometriosis affects at least 11% of women and diagnosis takes on average 4-7 years between symptom presentation and disease diagnosis.

source.title: Endometriosis diagnosis, staging and typology and adverse pregnancy outcome  history
source.authors: Schliep KC, Farland LV, Pollack AZ, Buck Louis G, Stanford JB, Allen-Brady K, Varner MW, Kah K, Peterson CM

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Endometriosis affects at least 11% of women and is an important marker of female fecundity. Diagnosis of endometriosis takes on average 4–7 years" Section: Background.

facts.claim: In the operative cohort, women with deep endometriosis (DE) had 46% live birth rate versus 69% in women without endometriosis; women with DE+OE had only 38% live birth rate.

source.title: Endometriosis diagnosis, staging and typology and adverse pregnancy outcome  history
source.authors: Schliep KC, Farland LV, Pollack AZ, Buck Louis G, Stanford JB, Allen-Brady K, Varner MW, Kah K, Peterson CM

facts.claim: Women with deep endometriosis had a 44% miscarriage rate among pregnancies, compared with 21% in women without endometriosis in the operative cohort.

source.title: Endometriosis diagnosis, staging and typology and adverse pregnancy outcome  history
source.authors: Schliep KC, Farland LV, Pollack AZ, Buck Louis G, Stanford JB, Allen-Brady K, Varner MW, Kah K, Peterson CM

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Miscarriage n (%) 134 (21) | 58 (25) | 17 (44) | 7 (26) | 6 (29)" Section: Table 5.

facts.claim: Endometriosis was diagnosed via surgical visualisation in the operative cohort using rASRM staging criteria, while the population cohort used pelvic MRI.

source.title: Endometriosis diagnosis, staging and typology and adverse pregnancy outcome  history
source.authors: Schliep KC, Farland LV, Pollack AZ, Buck Louis G, Stanford JB, Allen-Brady K, Varner MW, Kah K, Peterson CM

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Endometriosis was diagnosed via surgical visualisation in the operative cohort and pelvic magnetic resonance imaging in the population cohort" Section: Abstract Methods.

facts.claim: A systematic review of nine cohort studies found women with endometriosis had 1.75 higher risk of miscarriage (95% CI 1.29, 2.37) compared with women without endometriosis.

source.title: Endometriosis diagnosis, staging and typology and adverse pregnancy outcome  history
source.authors: Schliep KC, Farland LV, Pollack AZ, Buck Louis G, Stanford JB, Allen-Brady K, Varner MW, Kah K, Peterson CM

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "a recent systematic review of nine cohort studies finding a 1.75 higher risk (95% CI: 1.29, 2.37)" Section: 4.4.1.

facts.claim: Women with deep endometriosis were 2.98-fold more likely (95% CI 1.12, 7.95) to report a miscarriage compared with women without endometriosis after adjustment.

source.title: Endometriosis diagnosis, staging and typology and adverse pregnancy outcome  history
source.authors: Schliep KC, Farland LV, Pollack AZ, Buck Louis G, Stanford JB, Allen-Brady K, Varner MW, Kah K, Peterson CM

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Women with deep endometriosis were 2.98-fold more likely (95% CI 1.12, 7.95) to report a miscarriage" Section: Abstract Results.

facts.claim: A meta-analysis of random population-based studies found PCOS occurs in 10% (95% CI 7-14%) of all women.

source.title: Cyclic Progesterone Therapy in Androgenic Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS)-A  6-Month Pilot Study of a Single Woman's Experience Changes
source.authors: Shirin S, Murray F, Goshtasebi A, Kalidasan D, Prior JC

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "PCOS occurs in 10% (95% Confidence Interval (95%CI) 7-14%) of all women" Section: Introduction.

facts.claim: Two prior PCOS short-cycle (7- and 10-day) progesterone studies documented decreased serum LH and testosterone levels with progesterone therapy.

source.title: Cyclic Progesterone Therapy in Androgenic Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS)-A  6-Month Pilot Study of a Single Woman's Experience Changes
source.authors: Shirin S, Murray F, Goshtasebi A, Kalidasan D, Prior JC

facts.claim: Vaginal progesterone administration in physiological doses was associated with decreased LH levels and slowed LH pulse rates to normal in PCOS patients.

source.title: Cyclic Progesterone Therapy in Androgenic Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS)-A  6-Month Pilot Study of a Single Woman's Experience Changes
source.authors: Shirin S, Murray F, Goshtasebi A, Kalidasan D, Prior JC

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Vaginal progesterone therapy was also associated with decreased LH levels, and LH pulse rates slowed to normal" Section: Discussion.

facts.claim: Across studies including this one, withdrawal menstrual bleeding began within a day or two of stopping cyclic progesterone therapy.

source.title: Cyclic Progesterone Therapy in Androgenic Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS)-A  6-Month Pilot Study of a Single Woman's Experience Changes
source.authors: Shirin S, Murray F, Goshtasebi A, Kalidasan D, Prior JC

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "menstrual bleeding began within a day or two of stopping the progesterone" Section: Discussion.

facts.claim: This pilot study used a 14-day luteal-phase OMP regimen, longer than the 7-10 day protocols used in prior PCOS progesterone studies, to better mimic physiologic luteal phase exposure.

source.title: Cyclic Progesterone Therapy in Androgenic Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS)-A  6-Month Pilot Study of a Single Woman's Experience Changes
source.authors: Shirin S, Murray F, Goshtasebi A, Kalidasan D, Prior JC

facts.claim: The cyclic oral micronized progesterone (OMP) protocol used was 300 mg at bedtime on cycle days 14-27 (14 days per cycle) as sole therapy for a woman with PCOS intolerant of CHC.

source.title: Cyclic Progesterone Therapy in Androgenic Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS)-A  6-Month Pilot Study of a Single Woman's Experience Changes
source.authors: Shirin S, Murray F, Goshtasebi A, Kalidasan D, Prior JC

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "prescribed cyclic oral micronized progesterone (OMP) (300 mg/h.s. cycle days 14-27)" Section: Abstract/Methods.

facts.claim: Cyclic OMP therapy was associated with regular, shorter cycles averaging 28.2 ± 0.8 days, compared with the patient's prior ~35-day irregular cycles.

source.title: Cyclic Progesterone Therapy in Androgenic Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS)-A  6-Month Pilot Study of a Single Woman's Experience Changes
source.authors: Shirin S, Murray F, Goshtasebi A, Kalidasan D, Prior JC

facts.claim: Comparing cycles 1 and 6, fluid retention significantly decreased (p = 0.001), front-of-the-breast tenderness decreased (p = 0.002), and cervical mucus decreased (p = 0.048) on cyclic OMP therapy.

source.title: Cyclic Progesterone Therapy in Androgenic Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS)-A  6-Month Pilot Study of a Single Woman's Experience Changes
source.authors: Shirin S, Murray F, Goshtasebi A, Kalidasan D, Prior JC

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "decreased fluid retention (p = 0.001), breast tenderness (p = 0.002), and cervical mucus (p = 0.048)" Section: Abstract/Results.

facts.claim: Repeated measures ANOVA showed follicular phase fluid retention significantly decreased over time on cyclic OMP (F (1.2, 14.7) = 6.7, p = 0.017).

facts.claim: Combined hormonal contraceptives (CHC) are the current standard of care for PCOS, but all benefits except acne-related improvements largely disappear after six months off CHC.

source.title: Cyclic Progesterone Therapy in Androgenic Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS)-A  6-Month Pilot Study of a Single Woman's Experience Changes

facts.claim: In 765 menstrual cycles from 62 healthy women, self-reported fluid retention scores (0-4 scale) peaked on the first day of menstrual flow at a mean of 0.9 ± 0.1, not premenstrually as commonly assumed.

facts.claim: Endometriosis is a recurrent disease with an approximately 10% annual recurrence rate in the absence of medical therapy, which is directly related to the duration of time without treatment.

Section: Predicting recurrence and progression.

facts.claim: Hormonal therapies (GnRH agonists/antagonists, progestins, estroprogestins) do not cure endometriosis - symptoms often return after discontinuation and protective effects last only as long as they are used. These therapies are incompatible with both natural conception and ovarian stimulation cycles.

source.title: Risk of endometriosis progression in infertile women trying to conceive naturally or using IVF
source.authors: Somigliana E, Vigano P, Invernici D, Fornelli G, Merli CEM, Vercellini P

facts.verification_notes: Quote: 'Although these agents do not cure endometriosis and symptoms often return after discontinuation... Importantly, the protective effects of these therapies last only as long as they are used.' And: 'All established medical therapies for endometriosis hinder natural conception... medical therapy is incompatible with efforts to conceive naturally.' Section: Abstract + Introduction + The dilemma.

facts.claim: Ovarian reserve is critical for IVF success but does not influence natural conception chances - low ovarian reserve alone does not necessarily indicate a need to shift to IVF.

source.title: Risk of endometriosis progression in infertile women trying to conceive naturally or using IVF
source.authors: Somigliana E, Vigano P, Invernici D, Fornelli G, Merli CEM, Vercellini P

facts.verification_notes: Quote: 'Notably, low ovarian reserve does not necessarily indicate a need to shift to IVF, as ovarian reserve is critical for IVF success but not for natural conception (Zarek et al., 2015; Steiner et al., 2017; Galati et al., 2024).' Section: Predicting recurrence and progression.

facts.claim: During IVF ovarian stimulation, estradiol levels can rise to approximately 10 times those of a natural cycle (natural peak 200-300 pg/ml vs. strong IVF response up to ~3000 pg/ml).

source.title: Risk of endometriosis progression in infertile women trying to conceive naturally or using IVF
source.authors: Somigliana E, Vigano P, Invernici D, Fornelli G, Merli CEM, Vercellini P

facts.verification_notes: Quote: 'In natural cycles, estradiol levels peak at 200-300 pg/ml, whereas in cases of strong ovarian response, these levels can rise to concentrations 10 times higher (Macklon et al., 2006).' Section: The impact of ovarian stimulation on endometriosis.

facts.claim: Image quality varied substantially in the ENDO operative cohort: 52% of women with endometriosis had good-quality images vs. only 21% of women without endometriosis.

source.title: Interrater and intrarater reliability in the diagnosis and staging of endometriosis
source.authors: Schliep KC, Stanford JB, Chen Z, Zhang B, Dorais JK, Boiman Johnstone E, Hammoud AO, Varner MW, Louis GM, Peterson CM

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "52% of women with an endometriosis diagnosis had good quality images, while 21% of women without an endometriosis diagnosis had good quality images." Section: Materials and Methods.

facts.claim: When poor-quality images (n=50) identified by raters were excluded, computer-assisted staging reliability dropped from moderate to fair (Fleiss kappa 0.27, 95% CI 0.22-0.32).

source.title: Interrater and intrarater reliability in the diagnosis and staging of endometriosis
source.authors: Schliep KC, Stanford JB, Chen Z, Zhang B, Dorais JK, Boiman Johnstone E, Hammoud AO, Varner MW, Louis GM, Peterson CM

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "computer-assisted staging of endometriosis dropped from moderate to fair (Fleiss's κ=0.27, 95% CI 0.22–0.32)." Section: Results.

facts.claim: Surgeons reached identical revised ASRM staging in a mean of 61% of cases (range 52-75%) when reviewing the same operative images.

source.title: Interrater and intrarater reliability in the diagnosis and staging of endometriosis
source.authors: Schliep KC, Stanford JB, Chen Z, Zhang B, Dorais JK, Boiman Johnstone E, Hammoud AO, Varner MW, Louis GM, Peterson CM

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Surgeons agreed on revised ASRM endometriosis staging criteria in a majority of cases (mean=61%, range: 52–75%)" Section: Results.

facts.claim: Academic expert surgeons had practiced a median of 15 years post-fellowship (range 15-21) versus 5 years (range 2-20) for local specialized expert surgeons.

source.title: Interrater and intrarater reliability in the diagnosis and staging of endometriosis
source.authors: Schliep KC, Stanford JB, Chen Z, Zhang B, Dorais JK, Boiman Johnstone E, Hammoud AO, Varner MW, Louis GM, Peterson CM

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "academic expert surgeons had practiced nearly three times the number of years since their fellowship (median 15.0, IQR 15.0–21.0, range 15–21 years)" Section: Results.

facts.claim: Overall interrater reliability for endometriosis diagnosis (present/absent) across 8 surgeons reviewing digital images was substantial (Fleiss multi-rater kappa 0.69, 95% CI 0.64-0.74).

source.title: Interrater and intrarater reliability in the diagnosis and staging of endometriosis
source.authors: Schliep KC, Stanford JB, Chen Z, Zhang B, Dorais JK, Boiman Johnstone E, Hammoud AO, Varner MW, Louis GM, Peterson CM

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "with an overall Fleiss's multi-rater κ of 0.69 (95% CI 0.64–0.74)" Section: Results.

facts.claim: Academic expert surgeons had substantial interrater reliability for endometriosis diagnosis (Fleiss kappa 0.79, 95% CI 0.70-0.88), while local specialized expert surgeons had moderate agreement (Fleiss kappa 0.58, 95% CI 0.50-0.66).

source.title: Interrater and intrarater reliability in the diagnosis and staging of endometriosis
source.authors: Schliep KC, Stanford JB, Chen Z, Zhang B, Dorais JK, Boiman Johnstone E, Hammoud AO, Varner MW, Louis GM, Peterson CM

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "academic expert surgeons had substantial interrater reliability (Fleiss's κ=0.79, 95% CI 0.70–0.88)" Section: Results.

facts.claim: Interrater reliability for revised ASRM endometriosis staging was only moderate, with Fleiss kappa 0.44 (95% CI 0.41-0.47) for experienced clinical assessment.

source.title: Interrater and intrarater reliability in the diagnosis and staging of endometriosis
source.authors: Schliep KC, Stanford JB, Chen Z, Zhang B, Dorais JK, Boiman Johnstone E, Hammoud AO, Varner MW, Louis GM, Peterson CM

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "moderate interrater reliability when based on experienced assessment (Fleiss κ=0.44, 95% CI 0.41–0.47" Section: Results.

facts.claim: Intrarater agreement between expert clinical staging and computer-assisted revised ASRM staging was almost perfect, with approximately 90% identical classification (mean weighted kappa 0.95, range 0.89-0.99).

source.title: Interrater and intrarater reliability in the diagnosis and staging of endometriosis
source.authors: Schliep KC, Stanford JB, Chen Z, Zhang B, Dorais JK, Boiman Johnstone E, Hammoud AO, Varner MW, Louis GM, Peterson CM

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "approximately 90% of women identically classified (range: 83%–97%) and almost perfect agreement (mean weighted κ=0.95, range: 0.89–0.99)" Section: Results.

facts.claim: Academic expert surgeons rated lower endometriosis severity than local experts: 58% stage I, 24% II, 10% III, 8% IV vs. 47% I, 28% II, 16% III, 10% IV among local specialized experts.

source.title: Interrater and intrarater reliability in the diagnosis and staging of endometriosis
source.authors: Schliep KC, Stanford JB, Chen Z, Zhang B, Dorais JK, Boiman Johnstone E, Hammoud AO, Varner MW, Louis GM, Peterson CM

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "academic expert surgeons rated a lower incidence of stage II – IV (58% as stage I, 24% as stage II, 10% as stage III, and 8% as stage IV)" Section: Results.

facts.claim: Endometriosis was reported in approximately 66% of the 148 ENDO Study women with images, with little variation between rater types (65% academic vs 66% local specialized).

source.title: Interrater and intrarater reliability in the diagnosis and staging of endometriosis
source.authors: Schliep KC, Stanford JB, Chen Z, Zhang B, Dorais JK, Boiman Johnstone E, Hammoud AO, Varner MW, Louis GM, Peterson CM

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Endometriosis diagnosis was reported for approximately 66% of women, with little variation by type of rater" Section: Results.

facts.claim: Bland-Altman analysis showed limits of agreement between any two raters ranged from 38 points below to 49 points above (average 32 below to 33 above) for revised ASRM total endometriosis scores.

source.title: Interrater and intrarater reliability in the diagnosis and staging of endometriosis

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Limits of agreement between any two raters ranged from 38 points below to 49 points above, with an average of 32 points below to 33 points above." Section: Results.

facts.claim: With ovulation disruption alone, modeled effectiveness of levonorgestrel EC ranged from 49% (no delay) to 8% (72-hour delay between intercourse and EC administration).

source.title: Levonorgestrel emergency contraception: a joint analysis of effectiveness and mechanism of action
source.authors: Mikolajczyk RT, Stanford JB

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "With disruption of ovulation alone, the potential effectiveness of levonorgestrel EC ranged from 49% (no delay) to 8% (72-hour delay)." Section: Abstract Result(s).

facts.claim: With a 72-hour or greater delay, postfertilization mechanisms must be operative for LNG EC to obtain effectiveness of 50% or more, even assuming complete prefertilization inhibition.

source.title: Levonorgestrel emergency contraception: a joint analysis of effectiveness and mechanism of action
source.authors: Mikolajczyk RT, Stanford JB

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "a delay of 72 hours or more would require postfertilization mechanisms to be operative to obtain an effectiveness of 50% or more." Section: Results.

facts.claim: In vitro studies at LNG concentrations used for EC have not demonstrated effects on human spermatozoa function, acrosome reaction, or zona-binding capacity.

source.title: Levonorgestrel emergency contraception: a joint analysis of effectiveness and mechanism of action
source.authors: Mikolajczyk RT, Stanford JB

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Effects on human spermatozoa were not demonstrated in vitro for LNG concentrations as used in EC" Section: Introduction.

facts.claim: The discrepancy between LNG EC clinical-trial effectiveness and modeled ovulation-disruption effectiveness implies either inflated trial estimates or postfertilization mechanisms of action contribute to pregnancy reduction.

source.title: Levonorgestrel emergency contraception: a joint analysis of effectiveness and mechanism of action
source.authors: Mikolajczyk RT, Stanford JB

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "mechanisms other than disturbance of ovulation contribute to the reduction of clinical pregnancy, including mechanisms acting after fertilization." Section: Discussion.

facts.claim: At 96 hours or more after intercourse, the maximum possible ability of levonorgestrel EC to intervene before fertilization is essentially zero, because most women have already ovulated.

source.title: Levonorgestrel emergency contraception: a joint analysis of effectiveness and mechanism of action
source.authors: Mikolajczyk RT, Stanford JB

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "if LNG EC is taken 96 hours or more after intercourse, the maximum possible ability of LNG EC to intervene before fertilization is essentially zero." Section: Results.

facts.claim: In cycles with follicular diameter ≥18 mm at LNG-EC administration, only 2 of 17 LNG cycles avoided rupture within 5 days versus 2 of 16 placebo cycles, indicating minimal ovulation suppression near ovulation.

source.title: Levonorgestrel emergency contraception: a joint analysis of effectiveness and mechanism of action
source.authors: Mikolajczyk RT, Stanford JB

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Reduction factor for fecundity a 0.143 0.167 0.605" Section: Table 1 (12-14, 15-17, ≥18 mm follicle strata).

facts.claim: Mean follicular diameter before rupture in the modeling cohort was 21.7 mm (SD 2.8) with a relatively constant growth rate of approximately 1.5 mm per day.

source.title: Levonorgestrel emergency contraception: a joint analysis of effectiveness and mechanism of action
source.authors: Mikolajczyk RT, Stanford JB

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "the rate of follicular growth is reasonably constant across cycles (1.5 mm per day)... mean [SD] follicular size before rupture of 21.7 ± 2.8 mm" Section: Results.

facts.claim: The follicular growth equation derived from 533 ultrasound measurements in 327 cycles from 107 women: follicular diameter (mm) = 21.6747 − 1.5032 × (days to ovulation) + ε, where ε ~ N(0, 2.755).

source.title: Levonorgestrel emergency contraception: a joint analysis of effectiveness and mechanism of action
source.authors: Mikolajczyk RT, Stanford JB

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Follicular diameter (mm) = 21.6747 − 1.5032 × (Days to ovulation) + e" Section: Results.

facts.claim: Recent analyses suggest the overall effectiveness of LNG EC has been overestimated by approximately 10% (absolute difference) by the standard estimation procedures used in clinical trials.

source.title: Levonorgestrel emergency contraception: a joint analysis of effectiveness and mechanism of action
source.authors: Mikolajczyk RT, Stanford JB

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "the overall effectiveness has indeed been overestimated by approximately 10% (absolute difference)" Section: Discussion.

facts.claim: With a 24-hour or greater delay between intercourse and LNG-EC administration, mechanisms beyond ovulation disruption are required to obtain a clinical effectiveness of 50% or more.

source.title: Levonorgestrel emergency contraception: a joint analysis of effectiveness and mechanism of action
source.authors: Mikolajczyk RT, Stanford JB

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "With a delay of 24 hours or more, other mechanisms of action are required to obtain an effectiveness of 50% or more." Section: Results.

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The incidence of surgically visualized endometriosis for the operative cohort was 41%" Section: Results.

facts.claim: Lifestyle intervention reducing body weight by as little as 5% has been shown to have health, metabolic, reproductive, and psychological benefits in women with PCOS and excess weight and is considered the first-line treatment for overweight/obese anovulatory PCOS women.

facts.claim: Cross-sectional analysis of 311,064 prospectively measured menstrual cycles from 35,940 European and North American women aged 18-40 collected 2019-2021.

source.title: The menstrual cycle is influenced by weekly and lunar rhythms
source.authors: Ecochard R, Leiva R, Bouchard TP, Van Lamsweerde A, Pearson JT, Stanford JB, Gronfier C

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "35,940 European and North American women aged 18–40 years" and "prospectively measured menstrual cycles (311,064 cycles) over 3 full years (2019–2021)" Section: Abstract.

facts.claim: The lunar synodic month averages 29.5 days, divided in this study into 8 standard phases of 3-4 days each using the lunar package of R software.

source.title: The menstrual cycle is influenced by weekly and lunar rhythms
source.authors: Ecochard R, Leiva R, Bouchard TP, Van Lamsweerde A, Pearson JT, Stanford JB, Gronfier C

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "averaging 29.5 days in length" and "The lunar cycle was divided into 8 standard phases of 3–4 days each" Section: Abstract and Variables.

facts.claim: Menstrual onset occurred less frequently during Waxing crescent and First quarter phases than during Waxing gibbous, Waning gibbous, or Last quarter phases.

source.title: The menstrual cycle is influenced by weekly and lunar rhythms
source.authors: Ecochard R, Leiva R, Bouchard TP, Van Lamsweerde A, Pearson JT, Stanford JB, Gronfier C

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "first day of menstruation occurred less frequently in the Waxing crescent and First quarter than in the Waxing, Waning gibbous, or Last quarter" Section: Results.

facts.claim: Premenstrual spotting incidence is approximately 10% and was acknowledged as a potential limitation that could shift perceived menstrual onset.

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "the incidence of premenstrual spotting may be approximately 10%" Section: Discussion.

facts.claim: Higher progesterone levels after ovulation raise basal body temperature by approximately 0.5 degrees Fahrenheit.

source.authors: Ecochard R, Leiva R, Bouchard TP, Van Lamsweerde A, Pearson JT, Stanford JB, Gronfier C

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Higher progesterone levels after ovulation make the body temperature rise by approximately 0.5F" Section: Discussion.

facts.claim: A statistically significant circaseptan (7-day) rhythm of menstrual onset was observed with peak occurrence (acrophase) on Thursdays and Fridays.

source.title: The menstrual cycle is influenced by weekly and lunar rhythms
source.authors: Ecochard R, Leiva R, Bouchard TP, Van Lamsweerde A, Pearson JT, Stanford JB, Gronfier C

facts.claim: The circaseptan rhythm of menstruation was most pronounced for cycle durations between 27 and 29 days compared to shorter or longer cycles.

source.title: The menstrual cycle is influenced by weekly and lunar rhythms
source.authors: Ecochard R, Leiva R, Bouchard TP, Van Lamsweerde A, Pearson JT, Stanford JB, Gronfier C

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "This feature was most pronounced for cycle durations between 27 and 29 days" Section: Abstract Results.

source.title: The menstrual cycle is influenced by weekly and lunar rhythms

facts.claim: The EAGeR trial was a multi-center, double-blind, block-randomized, placebo-controlled trial evaluating low-dose aspirin effect on live birth in 1228 women with a history of one to two previous pregnancy losses.

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "placebo-controlled trial evaluating the effect of low dose aspirin (LDA) on live birth in 1228 women with a history of one to two previous losses" Section: Study Design and Population.

facts.claim: Pregnancy loss affects up to 15% of clinically confirmed pregnancies and an estimated 30% of all conceptions when very early losses are included.

source.title: Antimüllerian hormone and pregnancy loss from the Effects of Aspirin in Gestation and Reproduction trial

facts.claim: When presented with equal pregnancy rates and asked to choose between natural fertilization in the woman's body versus laboratory fertilization, 69% of U.S. respondents preferred natural fertilization (RRM) versus 17% for lab fertilization (IVF) in a nationally representative survey (N=1000).

source.title: Public Awareness, Perceptions, and Preferences in Fertility Treatment: Secondary Analysis of Two Public Surveys
source.authors: Parnell TA, Copeland K, Minjeur M

facts.verification_notes: Quote: 'Descriptive analysis indicated that 69% of respondents selected Option X, while 17% preferred Option Y. A Chi-Square test confirmed a statistically significant difference in the distribution of responses, χ² (2) = 573.16, p < 0.05, indicating a greater preference for Option X compared with Option Y.' Context: McLaughlin & Associates survey, N=1000. Option X = identify and treat underlying cause for natural fertilization; Option Y = bypass cause, lab fertilization. Pregnancy rates stated as equal.

facts.claim: In a U.S. national survey (N=1002), only 14% of respondents (n=145) claimed to be very familiar with IVF, while 13% (n=133) were not familiar at all and 31% (n=310) had heard of IVF but knew very little about it.

facts.claim: In a U.S. national survey (N=1002), 53% of respondents (n=531) preferred 'treating the root issue which is causing the symptom of infertility' over 'using IVF as a first line treatment' which was preferred by only 9% (n=86); 26% preferred a combination.

facts.claim: Endometriosis prevalence is significantly higher in infertile women (33%) than in previously fertile women undergoing sterilization (4%) based on studies published 1988-2000 (P<0.0001), with moderate-severe disease also more prevalent in the infertile group (32% vs 9%).

facts.claim: The monthly fecundity rate (MFR) in untreated women with minimal to mild endometriosis is approximately 2-4%, compared to an MFR of 15-20% in women with normal fertility, demonstrating significant fertility impairment even with mild disease.

Endocan study control group MFR: 2.4%. Berube 1998 estimate: <2.5% for min-mild endo vs ~3.48% for unexplained infertility.

facts.claim: A meta-analysis of 5214 IUI cycles demonstrated that the odds ratio for pregnancy in women with minimal-mild endometriosis was 0.45 (95% CI 0.27-0.76), as low as the OR for couples with male factor infertility (0.48, 95% CI 0.37-0.61).

facts.verification_notes: Quote: 'In a meta-analysis of 5214 cycles of high intrauterine insemination (IUI), using stepwise logistic regression, it was demonstrated that the odds ratio for pregnancy associated with minimal to mild endometriosis was 0.45, significantly reduced (95% CI, 0.27-0.76) compared with women without endometriosis and as low as the odds ratio (0.48) for couples with male factor infertility (95% CI, 0.37-0.61).'

facts.claim: The Canadian ENDOCAN randomized controlled trial (n=341) demonstrated that surgical excision of minimal-mild endometriosis nearly doubled the monthly fecundity rate (4.7% vs 2.4% control; rate ratio 1.9, 95% CI 1.2-3.1) and increased the cumulative pregnancy rate from 17.7% to 30.7% (P=0.006).

facts.claim: In oocyte donation studies, women with moderate-severe endometriosis who receive donor eggs from women without endometriosis have normal endometrial receptivity, while eggs donated FROM endometriosis patients to women with normal pelvis show reduced embryo quality and implantation rates, indicating endometriosis-associated subfertility is related to egg quality rather than endometrial receptivity.

facts.verification_notes: Quote: 'Women with moderate to severe endometriosis who receive donor eggs from women without endometriosis have a normal endometrial receptivity after IVF. In contrast, eggs donated by women with moderate to severe endometriosis to women with a normal pelvis seem to have reduced quality, resulting in reduced embryo quality and reduced implantation rate per embryo. These preliminary data suggest that endometriosis-associated subfertility is related to egg quality, not to endometrial receptivity.'

facts.claim: In the baboon model, monthly fecundity rate was significantly lower in animals with mild, moderate, or severe endometriosis than in those with minimal endometriosis or normal pelvis, with increased luteinized unruptured follicle syndrome suggested as a mechanism.

An increased incidence and recurrence of luteinized unruptured follicle (LUF) syndrome were observed in baboons with mild endometriosis.'

facts.claim: Within the fertile window, the type of mucus observed on the day of intercourse is more predictive of conception than the timing of intercourse relative to ovulation.

source.title: Mucus observations in the fertile window: a better predictor of conception than timing of intercourse

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "the type of mucus observed on the day of intercourse is more predictive of conception than the timing relative to ovulation." Section: Results.

facts.claim: Pregnancy in this study was defined as either an ongoing pregnancy of at least 60 days from the last menses, or a clinically identified spontaneous abortion within 60 days of the last menses.

source.title: Mucus observations in the fertile window: a better predictor of conception than timing of intercourse
source.authors: Bigelow JL, Dunson DB, Ecochard R, Gnoth C, Colombo B, Stanford JB

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "pregnancy is defined as either an ongoing pregnancy of at least 60 days from the last menses" Section: Materials and methods.

facts.claim: To maximize the likelihood of conception, intercourse should occur on days with optimal mucus quality observed in vaginal discharge, regardless of the exact timing relative to ovulation.

source.title: Mucus observations in the fertile window: a better predictor of conception than timing of intercourse

facts.claim: After exclusions for insufficient BBT data, no intercourse in fertile interval, or missing mucus information on intercourse days, the analysis included 1473 cycles with 353 pregnancies (out of 6724 cycles with 487 pregnancies total).

facts.claim: Mucus observations were classified on a 1-4 scale: 1 = dry/nothing seen; 2 = damp/nothing seen; 3 = damp with thick/creamy/sticky mucus; 4 = wet/slippery with transparent stretchy mucus.

source.title: Mucus observations in the fertile window: a better predictor of conception than timing of intercourse

facts.claim: Intercourse on any day in the 6-day fertile window with type 4 (most fertile) mucus had a pregnancy probability greater than 0.17, while pregnancy probability did not exceed 0.13 on days with no secretions (mucus score 1).

source.title: Mucus observations in the fertile window: a better predictor of conception than timing of intercourse

facts.claim: The day of lowest fertility within the 6-day fertile interval was 5 days before ovulation, and the day of highest fertility was 3 days before ovulation.

source.title: Mucus observations in the fertile window: a better predictor of conception than timing of intercourse

facts.claim: The gain in pregnancy probability from increasing mucus score from 1 to 4 (range 0.10-0.18) exceeded the gain from optimal timing of intercourse 3 days before ovulation versus 5 days before ovulation (range 0.06-0.14).

facts.claim: The posterior probability that mucus had no effect on pregnancy probability was less than 0.01, demonstrating a statistically significant trend of increasing pregnancy probability with each unit increase in mucus score.

facts.claim: In the ENDO Study operative cohort of 473 women undergoing laparoscopy/laparotomy, 40.2% (n=190) had a primary post-operative diagnosis of endometriosis, 31.1% (n=147) had other gynecologic pathology, and 28.8% (n=136) had a normal pelvis.

facts.claim: After complete laparoscopic excision of endometriosis in teenagers, zero of the 8 patients who underwent reoperation (47.1% of 17) had recurrent or persistent endometriosis diagnosed visually or histologically.

source.title: Complete laparoscopic excision of endometriosis in teenagers: is postoperative hormonal suppression necessary?
source.authors: Yeung PP Jr, Sinervo K, Winer W, Albee RB Jr

facts.verification_notes: Quote: 'The rate of repeat surgery was 8 of 17 patients (47.1%), but the rate of endometriosis (diagnosed visually or histologically) found at surgery was zero.' Context: Prospective case series N=17, follow-up up to 66 months (avg 23.1).

facts.claim: Only 35.3% (6/17) of teenagers took combination OCs for 10-22 months and 5.9% (1/17) took a GnRH agonist for 6 months postoperatively, yet still achieved complete disease eradication at repeat surgery.

source.title: Complete laparoscopic excision of endometriosis in teenagers: is postoperative hormonal suppression necessary?
source.authors: Yeung PP Jr, Sinervo K, Winer W, Albee RB Jr

facts.verification_notes: Quote: 'In the teenagers who had endometriosis excised, 6 of 17 patients (35.3%) did take combination OCs for 10-22 months, and 1 of 17 patients (5.9%) took a GnRH agonist (leuprolide acetate) for 6 months.' Patients were not specifically advised to take postoperative hormonal suppression.

facts.claim: 82.4% (14/17) of teenagers with histologically confirmed endometriosis had been on preoperative hormonal suppression and 76.5% (13/17) had previous laparoscopic surgery, demonstrating that neither suppression nor prior surgery rules out disease.

source.title: Complete laparoscopic excision of endometriosis in teenagers: is postoperative hormonal suppression necessary?
source.authors: Yeung PP Jr, Sinervo K, Winer W, Albee RB Jr

facts.verification_notes: Quote: 'Fourteen of these patients (82.4%) had been on preoperative hormonal suppression at the time of the index surgery at the CEC. Thirteen patients (76.5%) had previous laparoscopic surgery (range, 1 to 3 laparoscopies) for pain.'

facts.claim: In teenagers with histologically confirmed endometriosis, chronic pelvic pain affected 76.5% (13/17), dysmenorrhea 82.4% (14/17), and dyschezia 76.5% (13/17), indicating bowel-related symptoms are highly prevalent in adolescent endometriosis.

source.title: Complete laparoscopic excision of endometriosis in teenagers: is postoperative hormonal suppression necessary?
source.authors: Yeung PP Jr, Sinervo K, Winer W, Albee RB Jr

facts.verification_notes: Quote: 'the three most common symptoms were chronic pelvic pain described by 13 of 17 (76.5%), dysmenorrhea described by 14 of 17 (82.4%), and dyschezia described by 13 of 17 (76.5%) as moderate or more.'

facts.claim: 94.1% (16/17) of teenagers treated with complete laparoscopic excision had only stage 1-2 endometriosis by rASRM classification, indicating that mild-appearing disease is common in adolescents.

source.title: Complete laparoscopic excision of endometriosis in teenagers: is postoperative hormonal suppression necessary?
source.authors: Yeung PP Jr, Sinervo K, Winer W, Albee RB Jr

facts.verification_notes: Quote: 'The revised American Society for Reproductive Medicine (ASRM) staging of histologically confirmed endometriosis was stage 1 (5/17 or 29.4%) or 2 (11/17 or 64.7%), or 3 (1/17 or 5.9%).'

facts.claim: After complete excision in teenagers, dysmenorrhea dropped from 82.4% to 41.2%, dyschezia from 76.5% to 17.6%, painful exercise from 70.6% to 5.8%, and intestinal cramping from 58.8% to 5.8% (all statistically significant).

source.title: Complete laparoscopic excision of endometriosis in teenagers: is postoperative hormonal suppression necessary?
source.authors: Yeung PP Jr, Sinervo K, Winer W, Albee RB Jr

facts.verification_notes: Table 1 values (before -> after %): Dysmenorrhea 82.4 -> 41.2; Dyschezia 76.5 -> 17.6; Painful exercise 70.6 -> 5.8; Intestinal cramping 58.8 -> 5.8. Text: 'There were statistically significant decreases in symptoms by paired Student t-test in the following symptoms: dysmenorrhea, dyschezia, constipation, tender examination, painful exercise, intestinal cramping, and bladder pain.'

facts.claim: Published rates of endometriosis recurrence or persistence after ablation are 40-60% within 1-2 years, even with postoperative hormonal suppression, contrasting with 0% recurrence after complete excision in this teen cohort.

source.title: Complete laparoscopic excision of endometriosis in teenagers: is postoperative hormonal suppression necessary?
source.authors: Yeung PP Jr, Sinervo K, Winer W, Albee RB Jr

Authors cite Sutton 1994 and Winkel 2003.

facts.claim: Among eumenorrheic women without known subfertility, the reported per-cycle incidence of sporadic anovulation ranges from 1.0% to 14.5%, defined by LH surge or luteal progesterone.

source.title: Sporadic anovulation is not an important determinant of becoming pregnant and  time to pregnancy among eumenorrheic women: A simulation study
source.authors: DeVilbiss EA, Stanford JB, Mumford SL, Sjaarda LA, Kim K, Zolton JR, Perkins NJ, Schisterman EF

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "the reported per-cycle incidence of sporadic anovulation is highly variable, ranging from 1.0 to 14.5%" Section: Background.

facts.claim: In presumed fertile women with regular cycles, routine testing to ensure ovulation and treatment of sporadic anovulation are unlikely to be medically necessary.

source.title: Sporadic anovulation is not an important determinant of becoming pregnant and  time to pregnancy among eumenorrheic women: A simulation study
source.authors: DeVilbiss EA, Stanford JB, Mumford SL, Sjaarda LA, Kim K, Zolton JR, Perkins NJ, Schisterman EF

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "routine testing to ensure ovulation and treatment of anovulation are unlikely to be medically necessary" Section: Conclusions/Abstract.

facts.claim: Transvaginal sonographic detection found only one anovulatory cycle among 150 cycles in 53 women, suggesting true anovulation incidence is on the lower end of the 1.0-14.5% range.

source.title: Sporadic anovulation is not an important determinant of becoming pregnant and  time to pregnancy among eumenorrheic women: A simulation study
source.authors: DeVilbiss EA, Stanford JB, Mumford SL, Sjaarda LA, Kim K, Zolton JR, Perkins NJ, Schisterman EF

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "a single anovulatory cycle detected by transvaginal sonography among 150 cycles in 53 women" Section: Interpretation.

facts.claim: LH surge or luteal progesterone assessment may overestimate true anovulation by including suboptimal ovulation, and ovulatory quality may exist on a continuum.

source.title: Sporadic anovulation is not an important determinant of becoming pregnant and  time to pregnancy among eumenorrheic women: A simulation study

facts.claim: Time to pregnancy can be improved by either timing intercourse to the fertile window using biomarkers (LH, cervical fluid) or by increasing the frequency of unscheduled intercourse.

source.title: Sporadic anovulation is not an important determinant of becoming pregnant and  time to pregnancy among eumenorrheic women: A simulation study

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "biomarkers or cervical fluid may help time intercourse to the fertile window, time to pregnancy can also be improved through increasing the frequency" Section: Abstract.

facts.claim: In a simulation of 100,000 women over 12 cycles, reducing per-cycle anovulation incidence from 14.5% to 1% raised the cumulative pregnancy rate by only 4.0% (86.7% vs 90.7%).

source.title: Sporadic anovulation is not an important determinant of becoming pregnant and  time to pregnancy among eumenorrheic women: A simulation study

facts.claim: Increasing unscheduled intercourse frequency from weekly to every other day produced a 5-cycle median reduction in time to pregnancy (7 cycles to 2 cycles) in the simulation.

facts.claim: Mild endometriosis (rASRM stage I/II) carried a higher miscarriage rate (42.1%, 95% CI 32.6-51.4) than severe endometriosis (rASRM III/IV: 30.8%, 95% CI 22.6-38.7), with adjusted IRR 1.57 for mild disease (P=.046) but only 1.19 for severe disease (P=.446, not significant) vs controls.

source.title: Endometriosis, especially mild disease: a risk factor for miscarriages
source.authors: Kohl Schwartz AS, Wölfler MM, Mitter V, Rauchfuss M, Haeberlin F, Eberhard M, von Orelli S, Imthurn B, Imesch P, Fink D, Leeners B

facts.claim: Superficial peritoneal endometriosis (SUP) had the highest miscarriage rate (42.0%, 95% CI 32.0-53.9) with adjusted IRR 1.67 (95% CI 1.07-2.61, P=.024), higher than ovarian endometrioma (28.6%, adjusted IRR 1.11 NS) or deep infiltrating endometriosis (33.9%, adjusted IRR 1.19 NS).

facts.verification_notes: Table 3 phenotypic classification. Superficial lesions had the only statistically significant adjusted IRR among phenotypes. Authors interpret this as evidence that mild-active inflammatory disease impairs reproduction more than severe/scarring disease.

facts.claim: In subfertile women with endometriosis, the miscarriage rate was 50.0% (95% CI 40.7-59.4) compared to 25.8% in subfertile control women (adjusted IRR 2.41, 95% CI 1.01-5.78, P=.048), while fertile women with endometriosis showed no difference from fertile controls (24.5% vs 21.5%, P=.548).

facts.claim: Zero minimum requirement for endometriosis or tubal surgery in REI fellowship; 45% want more surgical training
facts.body: ACGME training requirements for Reproductive Endocrinology and Infertility (REI) fellowship include zero minimum cases for endometriosis surgery or tubal surgery. 45% of REI trainees report wanting additional surgical training, but fellowship programs lack faculty to teach it. Many REI programs refer for reproductive surgery to general gynecologists or MIGS-trained surgeons. Conversely, MIGS (Minimally Invasive Gynecologic Surgery) fellowship has no or minimal fertility-focused training, with emphasis on pain management and hysterectomy. General OBGYN residency also has minimal focus on fertility treatments, adhesion prevention, and endometriosis treatment. This creates a training void that NaPro/RRM surgical fellowship specifically addresses.

facts.claim: RRM treatment is reusable for many cycles and future pregnancies
facts.body: RRM treatment addresses underlying health conditions, so improvements persist across future pregnancies without repeating the treatment. IVF is per-cycle -- each attempt is a new treatment episode with full cost and risk exposure. RRM providers often practice obstetrics and follow patients through pregnancy and delivery. REI doctors typically discharge patients back to a regular OBGYN around the second trimester. RRM can also be used preconceptionally in young women to prevent infertility, while conventional medicine requires waiting until infertility is diagnosed before treatment begins. RRM only needs one egg per cycle (optimized), vs. IVF wanting 10-20 eggs (many will be small/poor quality, especially in older ovaries).

source.raw_source_id: Whittaker NM, RRM vs ART lecture

facts.claim: Over 75% of partially obstructed fallopian tubes normalized with catheterization; pregnancy rates 9-37%
facts.body: Transcervical catheterization during selective HSG successfully normalized intratubal pressure in over 75% of partially obstructed fallopian tubes (441 tubes studied), achieving pregnancy rates of 9-37%. ITP thresholds: freely patent tubes have significantly lower pressure than partially or completely obstructed tubes (p<0.0001); partially vs completely obstructed also differ significantly (p<0.0001). Completely obstructed tubes had an average ITP of 2.79 atmospheres and pressure remains unchanged after TCFT. In partially obstructed tubes, TCFT significantly lowers ITP to normal (p<0.0001). Histopathologic examination of 25 completely obstructed tubes showed specific organic pathology in all cases: salpingitis isthmica nodosa, endometriosis, and luminal stenosis (multiple diagnoses in some cases). In partially obstructed tubes, an amorphous material 'plug' in the proximal tube is often the cause -- explaining why catheterization normalizes pressure so effectively and why regular HSG sometimes shows initial blockage followed by delayed passage (previously misattributed to 'tubal spasm'). A 61% bilateral catheterization success rate has been reported. Gleicher's reference ranges: normal ITP 0.05-0.46 ATM; diseased oviducts 1.24 +/- 0.58 ATM. Radiation exposure is comparable to standard HSG.
facts.verification_notes: Promoted from local rrm-cli facts.json (170 curated facts)

facts.claim: Selective HSG with the ability to canalize blocked tubes
facts.body: Selective HSG (sHSG, also written SHSG) and regular HSG are fundamentally different procedures that should never be conflated. Regular HSG floods contrast through the uterus and provides only a binary pass/fail assessment of tubal patency with a 25% false positive rate for tubal blockage. It cannot detect partial occlusions or measure tubal function. Selective HSG uses direct catheterization into each tubal ostium under live fluoroscopy (C-arm), allowing measurement of intratubal pressure (ITP) via a LeVeen pressure-monitoring syringe to detect partial occlusions invisible to regular HSG. When ITP is elevated (>=0.7 ATM), a Jansen-Anderson 0.018-inch flexible-tipped guidewire is inserted through a 3F delivery catheter to clear the tube (transcervical catheterization of the fallopian tubes, TCFT). sHSG is both diagnostic AND therapeutic in the same procedure; regular HSG is diagnostic only. Timing: early proliferative phase (cycle days 4-8), under general anesthesia (with laparoscopy) or IV sedation (standalone). In NaPro/RRM practice, sHSG has completely replaced regular HSG. Dr. Naomi Whittaker describes sHSG as 'one of the simplest and most effective high yield treatments' and often performs it simultaneously with laparoscopy -- she notes endometriosis patients tend to have more tubal occlusions and prefers patients be asleep for optimal focus. sHSG can also be performed through the hysteroscope when standard transcervical catheterization fails. First described by Corfman and Taylor (1966); standardized by Gleicher et al.; ITP measurement protocol by Hilgers and Yeung (1999, Fertility and Sterility). NaProTECHNOLOGY textbook Ch. 66.

source.raw_source_id: Hilgers TW, NaProTECHNOLOGY textbook Ch. 66, 2004; Hilgers TW, Young DA, Fertility and Sterility
